description: In the heart of downtown Healdsburg, one of the country’s most accomplished chefs Kyle Connaughton, along with farmer and wife Katina Connaughton, host guests at SingleThread Farm-Restaurant-Inn, a 55-seat fine dining restaurant with five exclusive suites above the restaurant. The farm, located on 24 acres in the historic Dry Creek Valley, supplies vegetables, fruit, herbs, flowers and honey, to their restaurant. Guests of the restaurant are treated to a 10-course tasting menu, showcasing the seasonal bounty from the couple’s farm. SingleThread provides guests a bespoke hospitality experience in the center of Sonoma Wine Country.

name: Chez Noir
description: Chez Noir is Jonny and Monique’s love letter to Monterey County, celebrating its finest ingredients. A Michelin-starred restaurant in Carmel-by-the-Sea, Chez Noir offers a seasonal tasting menu rooted in coastal California cuisine. The experience is centered around a refined, multi-course dinner that reflects the surrounding landscape, with an emphasis on seafood, thoughtful sourcing, and a deep sense of place.Designed for intimate evenings and special occasions, it is well suited for celebrations, anniversaries, and memorable dinners. Guests can enjoy the warmth of the dining room or outdoor patio seating, with a menu that evolves regularly and is complemented by a curated wine program.Chez Noir is designed for adults. We leave the decision to bring children up to the parents, we do not offer off-menu dishes or high chairs. We do not have a phone; for more info, email info@cheznoircarmel.com. Reservations are released on the 1st of the preceding month at 9am PST.

name: PRESS Restaurant
description: PRESS Restaurant celebrates Napa Valley — the people, the ingredients, and the wines. PRESS is a place for locals, winemakers, vintners, and guests from around the world to experience the best the Napa Valley has to offer — it’s this sense of community that is at the heart of everything PRESS does. Under the leadership of Samantha Rudd and Chef Philip Tessier, PRESS features legendary Bay Area purveyors alongside innovative new culinary talents to highlight Napa Valley cuisine. The PRESS Culinary Team draws inspiration from around the globe while staying rooted in the style and ingredients that make the Napa Valley so special. Following through on the Rudd family’s vision, PRESS is home to the largest collection of Napa Valley wines in the world. The Napa Valley-focused wine list celebrates current vintages as well as older wines going back over 70 years with over 2,700 unique selections. PRESS received its first Michelin Star in 2022 and the Wine Spectator Grand Award in 2023.

name: Ella Dining Room and Bar
description: From the family behind The Kitchen, OBO' Italian Table & Bar, and Sellands Cafe & Bar, Ella Dining Room and Bar is a Downtown Sacramento landmark and premier dining destination. Ella offers New American, farm-to-fork cuisine for lunch and dinner, featuring seafood, steaks, pastas, small plates, fresh oysters, and traditional caviar service. Guests can also enjoy wine from an award-winning wine list and seasonal, hand-crafted cocktails at Ella's renowned bar.Ella specializes in private parties, with versatile spaces for gatherings of all sizes and a dedicated events team to help plan every detail.Located within walking distance of the Convention Center, State Capitol, Golden 1 Center, and major theaters and hotels, Ella also provides convenient valet service for shows and events.

name: Canon
description: Michelin Guide rated neighborhood restaurant with creative and shareable cuisine and a year-round patio."With a clear vision, a striking aesthetic and distinctive, mouthwatering flavors, Canon restaurant in East Sacramento is a stellar and welcome addition to the city’s dining scene." - 4-Stars, Sacramento Bee "Find the convivial Canon sequestered away from the downtown bustle. This cool space that contrasts deep tones against bright whites is enveloped by the aromatic smell of smoke, so it’s no surprise that the house is packed most nights. Chef Brad Cecchi is the culinary mastermind and his menu is pivoted around playful small plates." - Michelin Guide

name: Camden Spit and Larder
description: Camden Spit & Larder, a modern London-inspired eatery, in Downtown Sacramento on Capitol Mall. Chef Oliver Ridgeway showcases the pristine ingredients of the surrounding region with its bold design, bustling bar scene, and prime private dining facilities. Camden is comfortable yet elevated. We are located just a few blocks from the Capitol and Convention Center and just steps from the Golden 1 Center. This is the perfect place to dine pre-show or concert. Camden S&L was a restaurant 'Recognized by Michelin' in 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024 & 2025.

name: The Garden Room
description: Welcome to our Garden. A place to celebrate, unwind and step away from the noise of life. An unexpected escape from the routine. Experience an unforgettable chef-driven cuisine and indulge in innovative cocktails while surrounded by lush florals that warmly welcome you. The sense of community and euphoria will captivate one’s sense of belongingness. A 15 minute grace period is allocated to each reservation, before cancellation. Kindly note, all guests in your party must arrive prior to being seated. Valet parking is available at St. Regis Atlanta. The cost is $20 after validation. Please validate your ticket at the host stand. *We are unable to validate self-parking.

name: Yebo Beach Haus
description: Yebo Beach Haus is a trendy beach bungalow restaurant that provides a fresh take on New American Coastal Cuisine. Situated in the heart of Buckhead, the restaurant derives decor inspiration from various beach locations around the world, where diners often comment on feeling as though they have escaped to places like Los Angeles, Miami and Cape Town upon entry. Popular dishes like the Tuna Tataki, Red Butter Curry Halibut and Ostrich Bolognese showcase Yebo's unique and boldly flavorful twist on global dishes. The space includes a serene courtyard patio, large private dining room and plentiful indoor and outdoor bar seating. To enhance the dining experience for all of our guests, we ask that you respect our elegant dining dress code. We encourage that sweatpants, athletic attire, flip flops, shorts and cropped tops not be worn.Baseball hats are not permitted.

name: Lazy Betty
description: Lazy Betty is a Michelin-starred restaurant in Atlanta's Midtown, offering a unique culinary experience led by Chef Ron Hsu. The modern and elegant ambiance is perfect for special occasions or refined indulgence.The menu features a blend of modern American and New American cuisine, influenced by Chef Hsu's Chinese heritage. Guests can choose from two tasting menus that highlight seasonal ingredients and innovative techniques.Designed by Blue Lantern Studios, the interior creates a warm and inviting atmosphere with earth tones, organic materials, and natural light. The dining room includes teal velvet banquettes and terracotta plaster walls, while the bar and lounge provide a relaxed setting with greenery and brass accents.Lazy Betty is also committed to inclusivity, offering a sensory-friendly dining experience for guests with autism, PTSD, and other sensitivities, ensuring everyone can enjoy their meal comfortably.

name: The Optimist
description: The Optimist brings a seaside fish camp experience to landlocked Atlantans. The ever-changing menu features high-quality, sustainable seafood masterfully paired with fresh, local ingredients. From sophisticated, daily-catch dishes to crudos and beachside classics, chef-owner Ford Fry’s The Optimist transports guests to their favorite coastal haunts.

name: Little Sparrow
description: Helmed by Executive Chef Bob Ryan, Little Sparrow is a chic neighborhood spot from restaurateur Ford Fry. Inspired by Parisian bistros and Brooklyn’s favorite taverns, Little Sparrow's indulgent menu presents timeless Brasserie classics and delicious, seasonal fare. Dinner is framed by a top-flight cocktail program and a superbly curated wine list, as well as tableside service meant to make every guest feel pampered.

name: Ray's on the River
description: For over 40 years, Ray's on the River has been an Atlanta landmark—a place where the wild elegance of the Chattahoochee River meets genuine, heartfelt hospitality. Established in 1984, our intimate dining rooms, patios, riverside lawn and scenic gardens offer a setting unlike any other in the city.  Enjoy hand-cut steaks and the freshest catches, paired with a master cocktail program and an award-winning wine list—all with stunning panoramic views of the Chattahoochee. Whether you're celebrating a special occasion, hosting a private event, or simply enjoying an evening on the river, our team is at your service. We can't wait to welcome you.

name: Canoe
description: Canoe is located in Atlanta's historic Vinings area on the Chattahoochee River where Buckhead meets Vinings.Canoe's beautiful historic riverside setting makes it the perfect spot for weddings, receptions and private celebrations.Its original cuisine and distinctive design have already been featured in Bon Appetit, Food And Wine, Gourmet, The Wine Spectator and The New York Times.  Canoe, recipient of the prestigious Mobil 4 Star Award, was also selected as one of the "Best New Restaurants" in the country by the James Beard Foundation.

name: Füm
description: Füm is a contemporary Italian restaurant rooted in craft, fire, and community. We make food the slow way – honoring time & technique. Our pastas are handmade every day, using the best ingredients we can source. Flour, eggs, time & repetition – no shortcuts, no boxes. Just craft, muscle memory and tradition. Cooking with live fire shapes everything we do – how food tastes, how the room breathes and how our people connect. Our open hearth is the heart of our kitchen, grounding the space with warmth, sound and movement. We dry-age our own meats because time transforms everything. Flavor develops in stillness and restraint is its own form of skill.At the bar, we approach cocktails with the same mindset and philosophy as our food – balance, clarity and intention. No fuss, no theatrics - just clean, confident cocktails made for our grandioso bar.

name: Mujō
description: Mujō is a one MICHELIN star, intimate, omakase-only tasting menu experience serving modern edomae sushi led by Executive Chef J. Trent Harris.  Our menu changes daily and reflects the best seasonal ingredients available, as well as fish flown in directly from Japan.  The experience consists of a series of small plates and a progression of seasonal nigiri.  We are inspired by the Japanese concept of ichi-go ichi-e, 一期一会, or “one time, one meeting.  Our goal is not to recreate the sushiya of Japan, but to offer an experience that represents who and where we are, while respecting and honoring the traditions of edomae sushi. Inspired by the concept of omotenashi, but rooted in southern hospitality.

name: Rabbit Ears
description: Rabbit Ears is a cocktail bar pouring with intention, balance, clarity and craft. No gimmicks, no shortcuts. Inventive drinks meet a curated DJ program and world-class sound, all framed by sweeping panoramic views of Atlanta's skyline. Equal parts sophisticated and transportive, it's a late-night escape where the city itself becomes part of the performance. Come for the drink. Stay for the record.

name: Red Ash Italia
description: Wood Fired hand crafted Italian food set in a rustic modern open space in the warehouse district in ATX. A wide range of specialty dishes created by Chef owner John Carver and his staff on a nightly basis. Our house made Pastas, Fresh Seafood, and Prime Steaks are all prepared on wood fired grill, oven and plancha. An International wine list, specialty craft cocktails, and local brewed beers to choose from. Dolce and some Grappa, Amaro or Vino Dolce to add to your dining experience at Red Ash Italia. // We happily accept Visa, Mastercard, Discover, American Express and cash. We are unfortunately not able to accept tap to pay.

name: Este
description: Nestled between East Austin neighborhoods, Este celebrates fresh seafood, Mexican cuisine, and charcoal cooking. From the team behind the East 6th favorite, Suerte, Chef Fermin and restaurateur Sam Hellman-Mass are taking their love for masa coastal. In a location with a rich history, Este’s bright dining spaces, artisan decor and spacious outdoor deck bring the energy of seaside towns to East Austin. An extensive wine list and vibrant coastal-inspired cocktails are the perfect pairing for aguachiles, ceviches, oysters y otros mariscos!

name: Clark's Oyster Bar - Austin
description: Clark's features classic American seafood with an emphasis on freshness, local products, simple preparation, attention to detail, and clean presentation.  Classic coastal cooking from the Texas Gulf to the Northeastern Seaboard influences Clark's menu.  A large portion of the concept is devoted to oysters, raw bar, seafood, and caviar. We procure the freshest oysters from small harvesters around North America and serve cold seafood platters featuring super fresh daily specials.Learn more about MML Hospitality and their family of restaurants at www.mmlhospitality.com.

name: Suerte
description: Suerte is a restaurant inspired by the people, cooking techniques, and flavors of Mexico. Nationally acclaimed Chef Fermin Nunez and his team pride themselves on the use of local ingredients from our beloved farmers to achieve something delicious and uniquely East Austin.

name: Hestia
description: Hestia is downtown Austin's Michelin-starred live fire restaurant. A custom 20-foot hearth anchors the dining room and nearly every dish is shaped by flame. Choose from a 12-course tasting menu or à la carte. The wine program features 400 bottles with iconic producers and has been awarded the Wine Spectator Best of Award of Excellence since 2023.  Our sommeliers are on the floor every night to guide you. We accept reservations 60 days in advance. For parties over 6, please email us at info@hestiaaustin.com. Private dining and full buyouts available for groups.

name: Kemuri Tatsu-ya
description: Recognized by the James Beard Foundation, Michelin Guide and named one of the best restaurants in the U.S. by Food & Wine, Bon Appétit, GQ and more! Japanese izakaya and Texas barbecue are arguably the two best cuisines to enjoy while imbibing. Kemuri Tatsu-ya offers a mash-up of the two, plus an excellent selection of booze. Guests are welcomed into the space with an enthusiastic “irasshaimase!” from the staff. The experience only gets better from there with creative dishes like Taiyaki Cornbread, Brisket Tsukemen and Guaca-Poke. Bright, innovative cocktails, including super-chilled Toki highballs cranked out from a machine, arean essential part of the experience. Reserve a table today to get a taste of this iconic Austinrestaurant, brought to you by Tatsu Aikawa and the team behind Ramen Tatsu-Ya.

name: La Condesa
description: La Condesa is a two-times James Beard-nominated and MICHELIN Recommended Mexican restaurant in downtown Austin, Texas. Known for its modern take on traditional Mexican cuisine, La Condesa features locally sourced ingredients, bold flavors, handcrafted cocktails, and an extensive mezcal selection. From brunch and happy hour to dinner and private events, the restaurant offers a vibrant dining experience just steps from Austin's top hotels, nightlife, and entertainment districts. With its stylish atmosphere and nationally recognized culinary program, La Condesa remains one of downtown Austin's premier dining destinations.

name: Perla's Seafood and Oyster Bar
description: Perla's offers fresh fish and oysters flown in daily from both coasts with an emphasis on sourcing from the gulf and locations around the world. The bar makes delicious coastal cocktails and the wine list is stocked with fun sparkling, rosé & white wines.Perla's has Austin’s prettiest oak-shaded patio and a comfortable airy dining room featuring a huge saltwater fishtank, poured concrete oyster bar and open kitchen. Learn more about McGuire Moorman Lambert Hospitality and their family of restaurants at www.mmlhospitality.com.

name: Emmer & Rye
description: Named one of Austin's best farm-to-table restaurants, Emmer & Rye holds both a Michelin Green Star (for sustainability) and Bib Gourmand. The menu changes constantly and is driven by small Texas farms, ranchers, and foragers. Heirloom grains are milled fresh for house made pastas, breads, and desserts, whole animal butchery is done on site, and an extensive in house fermentation program captures flavors at their peak and preserves them for the off season. We accept reservations 60 days in advance. For parties over 6, please email us at info@emmerandrye.com. Private dining and full buyouts available for groups.

name: Birdie's  (Limited Reservations - Walk-in Always Available)
description: Birdie’s is a James Beard award winning and Michelin recognized walk-in only counter service restaurant with a prix-fixe menu. The restaurant will offer two reservations a night. If there’s no availability online, just walk in! We can always accommodate vegetarians. Please reach out to hi@birdiesaustin.com before you book if you have any other restrictions or allergies.

name: Nixta Taqueria - Austin
description: Hailing from Austin, Texas, Nixta is not a typical taquería. Owners (and lovebirds) Sara Mardanbigi and Chef Edgar Rico - a MICHELIN Young Chef recipient, James Beard Award winner, and TIME100 honoree - blend tradition and innovation to reshape Mexican-American cuisine. Nixta makes its own masa and tortillas from heirloom Mexican and Texan corn and is known for inventive signatures like its duck carnitas. Located near the cozy Chestnut neighborhood and not far from landmarks like the Texas State Capitol, Nixta offers a colorful, welcoming atmosphere with a sprawling backyard patio perfect for gatherings. You also can't miss the Nixta Jardín that won us a MICHELIN Green Star!Rooted in technique, history, and a true maíz revolution, Nixta delivers imaginative dishes and an unforgettable dining experience. So, if you're a lil' serious and a lil' silly, we got you.

name: Azumi
description: Opened in 2014, Azumi is located on Baltimore's Inner Harbor in the elegant Four Seasons Hotel. With picturesque panoramic waterfront views, Azumi offers authentic Japanese cuisine with a strong focus on sushi matched with an amazing "hot kitchen." Azumi's Sake and Japanese Whiskey program is one of the largest in the region. Named the city's best Japanese restaurant by Baltimore Magazine, Azumi is also one of the hottest spots for nightlife in the city featuring a live DJ on weekends. Enjoy outdoor dining on our expansive patio. Outside seating can be requested but not guaranteed.Azumi’s highly-skilled teppanyaki / hibachi chefs are on display in our FLAME ROOM as they cook the highest-quality meats, seafood, vegetables & rice on a solid-surface iron griddle right before your eyes in this intimate & interactive dining experience. Please call the restaurant to book parties of 8 and larger or for additional reservation assistance.

name: The Ruxton
description: Curated by Aaron Taylor, Atlas Restaurant Group’s Corporate Chef & Chef Partner in this concept, the menu puts steak in the spotlight with over 11 cuts from Chicago-based meat purveyor Meats by Linz. The Ruxton will serve a mix of wet and dry aged prime cuts of Black Angus Beef, as well as dry aging Long Island Duck and Bison from Virginia’s New Frontier Farm in-house. To complement the steak options, seasonal shellfish and seafood entrees will be available including dover sole, Maryland rockfish, whole live lobster, king salmon, Chilean sea bass and more.Looking to elevate your dining experience, book your reservation in our beautiful wine cellar here: https://theruxtonsteakhouse.com/the-wine-cellar-experience/The Ruxton will serve lunch every weekday and dinner 7 days a week. Be sure to visit  Atlas’ new world-class cocktail bar and lounge next door to The Ruxton called Order of the Ace.

name: MARTA
description: Tucked away in the charming heart of Butchers Hill community in Baltimore, MD, Marta stands out as a gem of culinary excellence where every dish tells a story of tradition and passion. Owned by Chef Matthew Oetting, a New York chef with over twenty years of dedicated culinary refinement, Marta is a celebration of authentic Italian flavors brought to life with a modern twist. Marta not only highlights their food and drinks, but have also created a place where locals gather, celebrate, and create memories, truly embodying the Italian philosophy of 'good food shared with great company.'

name: Cypriana - Homewood
description: For parties of 5 or more, please call us at 410-837-7482. From our very conception, Cypriana has been and will always be committed to providing clean, house-made and authentic Cypriot cuisine. A cuisine that is an amalgamation of the world’s wealthiest cultures. And we consider it a privilege to have started in one of the oldest cities in America. We predicate our image and pride on the familial hang-out environment and refined dishes that you can only get at Cypriana. It is our desire to bring a more traditional approach to full-service dining.

name: Amazonia
description: Venture into the relaxed ambiance of Amazonia, our inviting upstairs bar, dining room and rooftop hideaway. As one of the first Amazonian restaurants in the U.S., discover the exotic flavors and ingredients from Peru's rainforest, as well as favorite Peruvian staples. Here you can explore a thoughtfully curated selection of pisco and imaginative cocktails. The dishes you'll enjoy in Amazonia are meant for sharing, and are expertly prepared by the very same Michelin awarded kitchen team from Causa downstairs.We offer our Sour Hour happy hour at are bar and bar tables Monday thru Friday from open till 7 pm. Bar seating is available first come, first served. Reservations are not available for Sour Hour.

name: Dauphine’s
description: Dauphine’s is a modern day incarnation of the dramatic Grande Dame establishments of New Orleans, brought to you by the award winning Long Shot Hospitality - the team behind DC's beloved The Salt Line Restaurants. Dauphine’s utilizes Mid-Atlantic ingredients to prepare classic New Orleans fare while also creating original dishes inspired by the legendary city. All brought to you in a breathtaking spacious downtown restaurant setting with the most beautiful bar in downtown DC, plenty of patio dining, and a festive outdoor bar and garden area. Located in the Midtown Center, Dauphine's is just steps from the White House, National Theater, Convention Center, Cap One Arena and most downtown attractions and provides an elegant yet relaxed dining atmosphere for any occasion. Dauphine’s serves Dinner seven nights a week and Brunch on weekends, with Happy Hour every day 4pm-7pm and an ALL DAY Happy Hour on Sundays - plus don't miss our signature Sunday Live Jazz Brunch! We hope to see you soon!

name: El Taller del Xiquet
description: El Taller del Xiquet, or “Xiquet’s workshop,” celebrates Spanish flavors with a dedication to quality. Chef Danny crafts imaginative dishes from the finest ingredients, showcasing the vibrant cuisine of his roots. Our menu offers a seasonal selection of à la carte items and shareable plates, inviting you to explore the richness of both land and sea.Inspired by the traditions of coastal Spain, each dish reflects a respect for local agriculture and sustainable practices. This commitment is seen in premium proteins and seasonal produce from the Valencian region, highlighting the essence of Spanish gastronomy. Delicate octopus, sweet scallops, and succulent prawns are prepared with artistry and passion, while dishes like sustainable sea bass gravlax, signature paellas, and generous fideuàs are served tableside. El Taller del Xiquet offers a culinary journey into Spain’s traditions, honoring the ingredients and techniques that have graced tables for generations.

name: Xiquet by Danny Lledó
description: Xiquet (chee-KETT) by Chef Danny Lledó features a sought-after tasting menu showcasing the finest products from around the world served with warm, Mediterranean hospitality. Known as one of the best fine dining restaurants in Washington DC for its modern Spanish cuisine, Xiquet has held a MICHELIN Star since 2021 and earned MICHELIN’s Sommelier of the Year Award in 2022. In the same year, AAA honored Xiquet with the travel authority’s highest rating, making it the only Five Diamond restaurant currently in the city.  Recently, in 2023, the DC Restaurant Association awarded Xiquet with the RAMMY of Best Formal Fine Dining Restaurant of the Year.Please note that we do accept reservations for parties including 5 or more guests, however please contact us through email at info@xiquetdl.com and one of our sales managers will be in contact with you promptly to set your reservation up.

name: Bresca
description: Bresca offers A La Carte and Tasting Menu options! Parties of 5 or more will be required to do our Chef's Tasting Menu. We are a Contemporary French restaurant and cocktail bar located in the bustling 14th Street neighborhood.  Our team explores the finest in seasonal ingredients paired with genuine hospitality.

name: Thip Khao
description: Thip Khao is acclaimed chef Seng Luangrath's flagship restaurant in Columbia Heights, Washington, DC. Thip Khao serves up spicy Lao (or Laotian) dishes and classic cocktails with an exotic twist.  Lao cuisine leverages tropical herbs and cooking techniques of Southeast Asia.  However, the flavor profile is distinct from its fellow SE Asian countries like Thailand, Vietnam, and Malaysia. Dishes like Naem Khao (Crispy Rice Salad), Sai Oua (lemongrass pork sausage), Laab, and Tam Muk Houng (Lao papaya salad) at Thip Khao showcase the unique flavors of Laos.  Many dishes are served with sticky rice. Chef Seng recommends eating sticky rice with your hands as most Laotians do.  Since its opening, the restaurant has been well received by Washingtonians and critics alike. The restaurant has had national recognition from multiple publications including New York Times, Washington Post, Bon Apetit magazine, Washingtonian magazine, and Michelin Guide (Bib Gourmand).

name: Causa
description: Causa is our Michelin-Starred tasting room where the world of Peruvian culinary tradition comes to life, thanks to the artistry of Chef Carlos Delgado. Our current menu, a Journey Through Peru, highlights dishes and ingredients from various regions and sub regions throughout Peru. We are also now offering a vegetarian experience and happy to accommodate almost all dietary restrictions (gluten and dairy allergies cannot be accommodated) We offer an amazing 6 course wine pairing or non-alcoholic pairing as well.Reservations can be booked two months in advance. This prix-fixe menu is $179 (tax & gratuity not included). If you have any questions or clarifications on allergens or restrictions, please reach out to info@causadc.com.Gift Card? Please use a credit card for your reservation and we will refund you at the end of your experience. .

name: The Duck and the Peach
description: The Duck & The Peach is a New American restaurant in D.C.’s Capitol Hill, inspired by the fresh flavors of California and the warmth of New England. Led by Executive Chef Katarina Petonito and Rammy's Pastry Chef of the Year Rochelle Cooper, our seasonal menu features signature dishes like rotisserie duck with watercress panzanella and elegant, handcrafted desserts.Enjoy the full menu at our bar, offering craft cocktails and a curated wine list featuring women-made selections. Dinner is a celebration of the seasons, while our casual weekend brunch invites you to gather with friends and family. The space feels like stepping into a dear friend’s home—charming, warm, and welcoming.Join us at The Duck & The Peach, where every meal is a meaningful celebration of food, connection, and community. Named a James Beard Finalist in 2024 and one of D.C.’s 25 best restaurants by The New York Times, we can’t wait to welcome you.

name: Elmina
description: Bold flavors. Rich stories. Unforgettable culture. Elmina in Washington, DC is Top Chef finalist and Food Network star Eric Adjepong’s modern Ghanaian restaurant, inspired by the historic coastal town in Ghana. Here, West African tradition meets culinary innovation in dishes crafted with depth, meaning, and extraordinary flavor. Signature dishes include our Jollof Duck Pot and Egusi Stew Dumplings, reimagining West African classics with fine dining finesse. The atmosphere blends warm Ghanaian hospitality with modern elegance, accented by rich textures, natural woods, and vibrant art. Guests can enjoy intimate dining indoors or relax in our cozy outdoor patio, perfect for a romantic evening or lively gathering. Please be advised: For Friday and Saturday evening reservations for parties larger than 4, we offer limited availability. If you cannot find a suitable time, please call the restaurant directly.

name: Service Bar
description: Service Bar, opened by Chad Spangler and Glendon Hartley, has provided Washington DC with a relaxed and intimate alternative to the typical stiff-upper-lip cocktail bar.

name: Southbound
description: Widely known as one of the best restaurants in Charleston, the sound of crackling oak and aromas of freshly charred herbs and spices come to life at Southbound. Within the restaurant's old walls, every creak and whisper holds a promise of new connections, shared laughter, and untold adventures. At Southbound, you can enjoy a rotating menu which features dry-aged meats, perfectly balanced cocktails, and uncommon wines. Our live-fire kitchen invites you into its homey embrace, and creating a sanctuary where every bite and sip adds a chapter in the story of your evening.Dine at Southbound, and leave with a story of your own to tell.

name: Tempest
description: Tempest has been named the country’s #1 new restaurant by readers of USA Today’s travel guide, 10Best. Located on Market Street, in the former historic Harriott Pinckney Home for Sailors, next door to sister location Church and Union Charleston. Tempest features hyper-local, sustainable Coastal Modern-American food, with a seasonal menu curated by Chef Adam Hodgson and Bravo Top Chef Alum Chef Jamie Lynch.Tempest’s noteworthy raw and roast menu features both chilled and charcoal roasted options, with a variety of meats, pastas, fresh produce, and chef curated accompaniments that go beyond the sea, offering a taste for every palate.

name: Oak Steakhouse - Charleston
description:
Located on Charleston's historic Broad Street, Oak Steakhouse is a classic American steakhouse. A classic array of both wet and dry aged Certified Angus® Beef are featured on the menu. Ingredients are sourced locally and regionally from sustainable farmers and fisherman to accent the steakhouse menu. Items including local oysters on the half-shell, Clammer Dave's caper clams and a daily fresh fish selection depending on the season. Other popular dishes include the whole fried lobster, goat.sheep.cow artisanal cheese plate, wedge of Kurios Farms bibb lettuce salad, the house-made pasta and vegetarian plate.

The expansive and impressive wine program at Oak Steakhouse emphasizes big California Cabernets and Old World reds from Italy and Bordeaux with more than 200 wines by the bottle and 20 wines by the glass. The bar attracts a loyal, local following from nearby businesses and neighborhoods with its great cocktails and happy hour.

Inside a historic bank building that dates back to 1848, Oak's stately setting features three floors with five distinct dining areas. Arched windows, 20-foot ceilings, original metal work, fireplaces and 150-year-old heart pine floors, offers the perfect setting for enjoying a special evening.

name: Ma'am Saab
description: Ma’am Saab is a bold, modern Pakistani restaurant redefining the dining scene in Charleston’s historic district. Inspired by the vibrant energy of today’s Pakistan, our kitchen blends age-old recipes with contemporary flair, offering an experience that feels both rooted and refreshingly unexpected.Step into Ma’am Saab and you’ll find yourself on a flavor journey from the bustling streets of Karachi to the greenlands of Lahore, reimagined through a modern lens. Our menu pays homage to tradition while pushing boundaries, featuring craveable dishes like our signature Butter Chicken, with its rich and velvety golden curry; the off-menu Lamb Biryani, a slow-braised, deeply spiced classic (available in limited quantities nightly); the bold and fiery Chicken Karahi; and Taj-e-Sabzi, a crown jewel of seasonal vegetables elevated with spice and soul.Come for the food. Stay for the storytelling. This is Pakistani cuisine: modern, magnetic, and unapologetically flavorful.

name: La Belle Helene
description: From the team that brought you Church and Union Charlotte, Church and Union Charleston, Tempest Charleston, Church and Union Nashville,  Ophelia's Nashville and Church and Union Denver, La Belle Helene is a refined brasserie serving French fare, beer & wine in a soaring, elegant space with a lengthy bar.

name: Fin & Fino - Uptown Charlotte
description: Dive into a Sea of Flavor at Fin & FinoFin & Fino is a social seafood haven in the heart of Uptown Charlotte, where fresh flavors and vibrant energy collide. Indulge in a culinary journey across the sea with our menu of innovative small plates, featuring the finest catches and seasonal ingredients. From succulent oysters and housemade pasta to mouthwatering proteins and locally sourced produce, every bite is a celebration of the ocean's bounty.Our lively yet elegant ambiance sets the stage for an unforgettable dining experience. Whether you're sharing plates with friends, enjoying a romantic dinner, or celebrating a special occasion, Fin & Fino promises a night to remember.So, come embark on a culinary adventure at Fin & Fino, where the freshest flavors and vibrant atmosphere create a truly unique dining experience.

name: The Goodyear House
description: Welcome to The Goodyear House, nestled in the vibrant NODA neighborhood, just a stone’s throw away from Uptown. Led by the culinary prowess of Executive Chef Chris Coleman, a champion on Food Network’s Chopped, Beat Bobby Flay, and SuperChef, our restaurant promises an unforgettable dining experience. Indulge in our menu featuring casual elevated cuisine, expertly crafted to tantalize your taste buds. Whether you’re craving a hearty meal or a light bite, our diverse offerings cater to every palate. Relish the charming ambiance of our beautiful patio as you sip on handcrafted cocktails and savor delectable dishes. As part of NODA, the city’s premier arts and entertainment district, The Goodyear House invites you to immerse yourself in a culinary journey like no other. Book your table today and join us for an unforgettable dining experience.

name: Customshop
description: Customshop is a chef-driven restaurant that showcases local farms and purveyors in an effort to bring our guests the highest quality ingredients within our community. Executive Chef and Owner Andres Kaifer along with his team believe in crafting honest straightforward food with an elevated modern touch that delivers something truly unique. Weekly menu changes allow us to highlight the seasonality of food and the beauty of North Carolina produce, seafood, and specialty items. With stylistic inspiration from Spain, Italy, and France, our neighborhood restaurant aims to make everyone feel welcome. We are proud to offer a seasonal cocktail menu that also takes an ingredient-first approach and looks to mirror the creativity and ingenuity of our culinary team. Our wine list is curated around a balance of old-world staples as well as new and emerging producers pushing the envelope. We cannot wait to serve you on your next date night, post-work drinks with friends, or at a family celebration.

name: Rooster's Ballantyne
description: Chef inspired local restaurant specializing in Wood-fired cooking with local ingredients

name: Mita's Restaurant
description: Mita's is the second restaurant from James Beard Finalist Chef Jose Salazar and is named after his Colombian grandmother - his "Mita." The beautiful 130 seat restaurant designed by MSA Architects is located in the heart of downtown Cincinnati at the corner of Fifth and Race Streets on the ground floor of the 84.51 Building. Mita's eclectic, farm inspired tapas menu focuses on both the traditional and modern foods and beverages of Spain and Latin America, with charcuterie, ceviches, and paella.

name: Blue Point Grille
description: Blue Point Grille, located in the heart of The Historic Warehouse District, is renowned for serving the highest quality seafood sourced daily from both coasts. Its elegant dining rooms feature expansive windows offering sweeping views of the downtown skyline, creating a sophisticated ambiance. Just a short stroll from the convention center, premier hotels, and Public Square, guests can enjoy acclaimed dishes such as lobster bisque, freshly shucked Blue Point oysters, and the signature Nag's Head Grouper. Since its inception in 1998, Blue Point Grille has consistently been voted Cleveland's top seafood restaurant, known for its fresh seafood, award-winning wine selection, and exceptional service delivered by true professionals.

name: Edwins Restaurant
description: EDWINS Leadership and Restaurant Institute, led by Brandon E. Chrostowski, offers a unique dining experience with seasonal, classic French cuisine—now at the iconic Nighttown location in Cleveland.Founded in 2007, EDWINS' mission is to transform re-entry by providing formerly incarcerated men and women with leadership skills and a world-class education in the culinary arts.Dine with purpose and be part of changing lives through food and hospitality.À bientôt!

name: DANTE
description: Enjoy Michelin-starred chef Dante Boccuzzi"s signature restaurant DANTE. Located in a historic bank building in Tremont, DANTE restaurant has become one of Cleveland's premier dining spots. The decor features designs and works of art from local artists. Guests may choose from an a la Carte Menu or a Chef's Tasting Menu. Experience the excitement of dining in the kitchen as Chef Dante prepares and serves you a multiple course tasting menu.

name: GINKO
description: Irashaimase!!! We welcome you to enjoy Ginko Sushi Bar and traditional Japanese cuisine in Tremont. Our fish is always fresh, flown in from around the world, picked for Ginko. Ask about our daily specials and Ohio's largest Sake and Japanese Craft Beer offerings. We look forward to seeing you and walk-ins are always welcome!!!

name: Butcher & Rose
description: There's a place where legend intertwines with reality. A paradoxical union between power & elegance; craft & creativity. An unparalleled steakhouse experience brought to you by Cameron Mitchell Restaurants. This iconic location on Broad Street in Downtown Columbus showcases a harmonious blend of beauty and brawn with a playful, modern flair. We are delighted to introduce: Butcher & Rose - Now Open

name: Cento – German Village
description: A tribute to our 100th restaurant opening, Cento is an authentic, chef-driven Italian oasis. Inspired by the intimacy & culinary creativity of the finest restaurants in Italy, Cento is a warm, welcoming, & enchanting addition to German Village. Cento is a place for the entire community – a new, timeless, everyday destination where Great People Deliver Genuine Hospitality.Our patio seating is offered, weather permitting, on a first-come first-served basis. Happy hour is available at our indoor & outdoor bar-tops and indoor high-top tables only.If you cannot make your scheduled reservation, we ask that you please cancel by 12PM the day of your reservation. Cancellations outside the allowed window will be charged $25/person.  Valet offered daily starting at 5PM, $12 per car.

name: Tucci's - Dublin
description: At Tucci's, we believe that a memorable dining experience is incomplete without the perfect wine pairing. Our wine program reflects our commitment to excellence, featuring over 470 meticulously chosen labels for bottle service and a diverse array of forty-three wines available By the Glass. From renowned Italian vintages to exquisite international selections, our wine list caters to a wide range of tastes and preferences. We have recently expanded our dining room and significantly enlarged our temperature-controlled Wine Cellar to showcase our 2,500+ bottle inventory which shows our dedication to preserving the quality and integrity of our wines. Utilizing state-of-the-art Enoline machines, we ensure that wines served By the Glass maintain their freshness, allowing our guests to enjoy estate-bottled selections that are typically unavailable in this format.

name: Del Mar - Easton
description: Inspired by Mediterranean coastal cuisine, Del Mar features flavors from southern European countries, with a rotating and seasonal made-from-scratch menu that showcases a variety of seafood accompanied by fresh produce, house-made cheeses, rich olive oil and earthy spice blends. Handcrafted cocktails with refreshing flavors will pair perfectly with the dishes and invite guests to relax amongst a scene of lush greenery and European-inspired architecture.

name: Esh Modern Mediterranean - Fairfield
description: Esh offers a modern culinary experience inspired by the rich flavors and traditions of the Middle East, including Israel, Lebanon, Turkey, Greece, Morocco, and the Aegean. Our mission is to bring vibrant Mediterranean cuisine to life with a contemporary twist, celebrating the depth and diversity of the region through every dish.Please note: Parties of 6 or more must provide credit card details upon booking. Reservations must be canceled at least 24 hours in advance to avoid fees. If canceled less than 24 hours prior or in case of a no-show, a charge of $45 per guest will be applied to the card provided. Please note this fee is non-refundable. By confirming your reservation, you accept this policy. Final guest count must be confirmed at least 48 hours in advance.If your party increases to 10 or more guests after this window, our standard minimum spend policy will apply.

name: Sag Harbor Tavern
description: For many years I wanted to pay homage to my Grandmother Else who immigrated in the 1930s to my beloved neighborhood, Red Hook in Brooklyn, NY.I built my dream concept by tapping into my love of old NYC Taverns and imagined where my Grandmother would have wanted to go with family and friends and the Red Hook Tavern was born.Offering a timeless menu now known for its handmade pastas, spicy mussels and the legendary 45-Day Dry Aged Cheeseburger, alongside its coveted wine list, that guests come from all over the world to enjoy.Sag Harbor Tavern is a continuation of a tribute to my Grandmothers in beautiful Sag Harbor, NY. My Grandmother, Veronica was a Navy Nurse in WWII and a proud member of the American Legion. A building that we are humbled to now share with other proud Veterans.Sag Harbor is a special village with a great history and a wonderful community of wildly talented, hardworking folks. We are honored to be a part of this community and hope to do it proud.~Billy

name: The North Fork Table & Inn
description: North Fork Table is an East End institution. Set in a historic countryside home, the restaurant celebrates the bounty of Long Island’s farms, vineyards & waterways with menus by restaurateur & chef John Fraser. The restaurant’s menu focus is seasonally inspired featuring locally grown biodynamic and organic produce, the freshest seafood from the pristine Peconic Bay and Long Island Sound and award-winning North Fork duck and beef. Increasingly distinguished and awarded Long Island wines are highlighted on the wine list, as well as their national and international counterparts.

name: Mister Charles
description: Located in the landmarked former Highland Park Soda Fountain Building in Dallas, Mister Charles is Duro Hospitality’s most ambitious restaurant and bar. With irreverent takes on indulgent French and Italian dishes, elevated cocktails, rare wines, opulent over the top design and soaring 38-ft. ceilings, Mister Charles is redefining Dallas’ dining and tippling scene.Located at 3219 Knox St., Mister Charles is open for dinner seven days a week.

name: José
description: José brings a fresh, modern take on regional Mexican cuisine to Dallas’ beloved Lovers/Inwood neighborhood. Inspired by the brilliant and fast-paced culinary scene in Jalisco, the restaurant captures the serendipitous connections created when culture, food, tequila and art merge into one experience. Jose’s vibrant story comes to life at every table through its chef-driven plates, craft cocktails, the ceramics crafted by its renowned namesake, José Noe Suro of Ceramica Suro and the hand-drawn mural adorning the walls with scenes of Guadalajara and Tequila, which was a collaboration between Suro and Dallas-based designer and artist Megan Reinhardt. More than a restaurant, José is a community with authentically innovative Mexican food at its heart. Helmed by award-winning Chef Anastacia Quiñones-Pittman, José’s culinary program is seasonal, inspired and ever evolving.

name: Delilah - Dallas
description: Delilah Dallas makes its highly anticipated debut early an exclusive tasting preview presented by Chase Sapphire Reserve®  on the evening of Wednesday, January 28th 2026. To view availability for this event, add your eligible Chase card to your OpenTable account. Chase Sapphire Reserve® cardmembers are eligible for this event.  Delilah Dallas marks The h.wood Group’s fourth location of the famed roaring ’20s inspired supper-club. Situated in the heart of Texas, Delilah brings a new dimension to Dallas nightlife where Southern charm meets old-Hollywood glamour in a transportive, high-energy environment designed for elevated dining, socializing, and staying out well past midnight.

name: Nick & Sam's Steakhouse
description: Situated minutes from Downtown, Nick & Sam’s is a contemporary steakhouse featuring selections of prime meats as well as American and Australian Kobe and a variety of seafood. We offer a mostly American wine list of over 400 labels plus an international selection, with 20 wines by the glass.USA Today says “well-done”, ZAGAT “very good to excellent” and a 2008 recipient of the Five Diamond Star Award.

name: Sister
description: Sister is a restaurant project from Duro Hospitality. Sister will live in the halls of the famed Grape, a Dallas icon for over 40 years. She will serve loosely Italian-inspired neighborhood fare, wood-fired meats and fish, daily house made pasta, and lightly Mediterranean inspired cuisine. The design will feel like a family Trattoria that has grown through the years including collected memorabilia giving the space a quirky and playful feeling. Sister will be a relaxed and comfortable neighborhood spot for every day. Sister is open seven nights per week.

name: Tei-An
description: Chef Teiichi Sakurai, well-known as the founder of Teppo (1995) and Tei Tei (1998), opened Tei-An in 2008 at One Arts Plaza. Chef Sakurai continues the pursuit of introducing authentic Japanese culinary culture to Dallas through ancient techniques and years of mastering the fragile and sophisticated soba noodle. Called "The crown jewel of Japanese dining in Dallas" by former Dallas Morning News restaurant critic Leslie Brenner, Tei-An continues to induct diners into the art of dining at a traditional, yet modern, Japanese soba noodle house.

name: Town Hearth
description: Return to simple, primal, rustic style cooking. 6,000 Sq Feet, 1500 Bottle Wine Room, Old & New World Burgundies, Bottles from the Northwest. Sincere food that is real and straight forward, fun, and relatable. Design: Refined, Modern, Masculine, Rustic Vibe, Vintage Vein, progressively retro throwback appeal.Please call the restaurant if you do not see a reservation time you are looking for. We will do our best to get you a table! 214-761-1617

name: El Carlos Elegante
description: El Carlos Elegante is a Mexican-inspired eatery that is open for dinner service and showcases beloved Latin cooking traditions through the chef team’s talent and creativity. Patrons can enjoy large cuts of high-end, Argentinian-style wood-fired meats and fish, a stunning crudo selection, house-made tortillas, as well as traditional masa-based dishes relevant to the heart of Mexico. The restaurant also features a seasonal craft cocktail menu and a standout Mezcal program, as well as a global list of wines and champagnes.

name: Tavernetta Denver
description: Tavernetta brings Authentic Italian, Genuine Hospitality to Denver’s Union Station neighborhood, inspired by the food, wine, and culture of Italy. Led by Executive Chef Cody Cheetham, the menu features fresh handmade pastas and beloved signatures like Burrata with Pesto Trapanese, Rigatoni with Lamb Ragu, and classic Tiramisu.The Italian-focused wine list and thoughtful spirits program reflect the restaurant’s deep commitment to regional traditions. Tavernetta has been recognized by the Michelin Guide (Bib Gourmand), the James Beard Foundation, the World’s 50 Best Discovery List, and Wine Spectator.The dining room blends sleek, minimalist design with vintage Slim Aarons portraits and warm handcrafted details, creating a relaxed yet refined atmosphere. Guests can also enjoy the lively train-platform patio just steps from Union Station.Tavernetta offers a transportive Italian experience rooted in comfort, craft, and genuine hospitality.

name: Alma Fonda Fina
description: Chef Curiel makes his solo restaurant debut with Alma Fonda Fina, a restaurant dedicated to sharing the integrity, tradition and culturally rich experiences that defined his culinary journey and create a transportive dining experience that embodies the beauty of Mexico. As Chef Curiel continues to push the boundaries of taste and tradition, he pays homage to his roots and uses food as a way to showcase his cultural pride.

name: Rioja
description: Chef Jennifer Jasinski's creatively inspired food is influenced by local seasonal products.  Rioja is simple, pure food that emphasizes bright flavors and textures. The wines are a collection of fun interesting varietals that complement the cuisine.

name: KUMOYA
description: KUMOYA, is a Japanese Kitchen and Sushi Restaurant, the passion project of renowned sushi chef Corey Baker in collaboration with Max Mackissock, located in the heart of LoHi. We specialize in traditional sushi and innovative izakaya-style dishes with a modern Japanese twist. Our kappo-style dining concept allows guests to curate their own culinary adventure. Chefs Corey Baker and Max Mackissock draw inspiration from Kaiseki cuisine, celebrating the seasons and micro-seasons inherent in sushi and Japanese cooking, resulting in a dynamic, ever-evolving menu.AT KUMOYA, we prioritize technique, vibrant hospitality, and the use of exceptional ingredients—from the Japanese fish markets of Toyosu and Fukuoka to the finest Japanese and Colorado produce. Join us at Kumoya, where each dish tells a story, and every meal is a journey.

name: Ash'Kara
description: Ash’Kara explores the continuous evolution of Mediterranean cuisine, with ingredients and flavors from Europe, North Africa and the Middle East.

name: A5 Steakhouse
description: A great restaurant meal can be an act of storytelling and transportation. Sliding onto a stool at a bar, being met with a classy and silky martini punctuated with a briny olive, is there to prepare you for a plate of finely-shucked oysters that were bobbing in the Atlantic 36 hours ago. In this moment the noise and cacophony can melt away, your phone finds its way back into your pocket, and we find communion with those of us on our left and right.We place a premium on doing the work to find producers, growers, and ranchers that have values that align with our own. It’s our desire to bring food to your table that speaks to its sense of place and time, and to be able to bring the connection to those producers to a higher level of consciousness.

name: BRUTØ
description: BRUTØ is an 18-seat tasting menu restaurant focused on fermentation, preservation, and regenerative sourcing. Through technical process and research-driven cooking, the brutalist menu explores the intersection of sustainability and global food pathways - highlighting ingredients at their peak through thoughtful extraction, transformation, and full utilization.BRUTØ is located in the Downtown Denver Free Market. We have our own entrance off of 18th street, however you are welcome to enter through Free Market’s main entrance. We are open Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, and Sunday from 5:30 PM - 8:30 PM and Friday, Saturday from 5:00 PM - 8:30 PM.

name: Cozobi Fonda Fina
description: On a mission to share the beauty of Mexican culture through food, Chef Johnny Curiel opens Cozobi Fonda Fina as an encore to his beloved LoHi restaurant, Alma Fonda Fina. Named after Cozobi, the Zapotec god of corn, Cozobi Fonda Fina is rooted in Mexico’s centuries-old corn nixtamalization traditions and wood-fire cooking techniques that define its rich food history.

name: Four by Brother Luck
description: At Four, the rugged peaks of Colorado meet the sun-soaked deserts of the Southwest: regional cooking through the four seasons. Founded by chef-owner Brother Luck and led in the kitchen by Executive Chef Ashley Brown, the menu changes each season to follow what the Four Corners region grows, raises, and forages — from wild game and river fish to farm harvests and foraged finds. Choose a rotating four-course tasting or order à la carte, with craft cocktails, local beer, and a curated wine list. The room is casual and elegant; the cooking is serious. Chef-owner Brother Luck is a James Beard Semifinalist for Best Chef: Mountain, a Top Chef and Beat Bobby Flay alum, and author of No Lucks Given.We offer happy hour Tuesday - Sunday from 3pm-6pm, with discounted drinks and bar appetizers. Dinner service begins at 5pm Tuesday - Sunday.Vegan 4-course Menu Offered in addition to regular menu

name: Frasca Food and Wine
description: Located steps from Pearl Street Mall, Frasca Food and Wine is a Michelin-Starred, James Beard Award–Winning restaurant celebrating the cuisine and hospitality of Friuli-Venezia Giulia, Italy. Founded by Master Sommelier Bobby Stuckey and Chef Lachlan Mackinnon-Patterson, and led by Chef Ian Palazzola, Frasca brings refined Italian dining to downtown Boulder.Enjoy signature dishes like the beloved Frico Caldo, the immersive Chef’s Table, or the Friulano Tasting Menu, paired with wines from an award-winning list spanning Friuli’s native grapes to world-renowned cult labels.With elegant interiors, warm service, and a legacy of excellence—including a Michelin One Star and the 2025 James Beard Award for Outstanding Restaurant—Frasca offers one of Colorado’s most exceptional dining experiences. Perfect for special occasions, fine wine dinners, or romantic evenings in Boulder.Gift cards available at frascafoodandwine.com.

name: Oak & Reel
description: Dining at Oak & Reel is a journey – We source the freshest seafood, kissed by flames fueled by our oak burning hearth, paired with house made pasta, and wrapped in a stunning yet casual-contemporary environment. Expect bright, clean, bold flavors and a carefully curated wine and cocktail list to complement the menu. Our commitment is to bring family, friends and neighbors together for an unforgettable dining experience.

name: BARDA
description: Inspired by the parrillas of his native Argentina and the hip enclaves of contemporary Buenos Aires, Chef Javier Bardauil brings the art of live-fire cooking to Detroit at BARDA — a modern steakhouse where ancestral traditions meet out-of-this-world innovation. The BARDA kitchen relies solely on a wood-burning grill and oven, ensuring that every dish is either flame-cooked, fire-kissed, or served raw, showcasing premium cuts of meat and high-quality seafood and vegetables prepared to highlight their natural flavors. The thoughtfully curated South American wine list and playful cocktail menu provide artful balance to the fire-infused cuisine. Recognized as a James Beard Award finalist for Best New Restaurant in 2022, Barda invites you to experience the depth and warmth that only live-fire cooking can offer, in a futuristic space unlike any other restaurant in America.

name: Bar Pigalle
description: Bar Pigalle, located at 2915 John R Street in the heart of Brush Park, Detroit, offers a New-American, French-inspired dining experience combined with a creative selection of playful cocktails and an exceptional wine list. Led by award-winning mixologist Travis Fourmont and Chef Norman Valenti, Bar Pigalle is nestled within The Carlton Lofts, a historic building designed by renowned American architect Louis Kamper.

name: Baobab Fare
description: A celebration of East African  food & culture.Bringing the vibrant flavors of East Africa to Detroit, Baobab Fare is a restaurant founded by refugees from Burundi that embodies the spirit of “Detroit Ni Nyumbani” — Detroit is Home. Baobab Fare serves 100% halal homestyle East African dishes in a bright, welcoming space that also offers coffee, tea, and fresh juices. All of our desserts are vegan and much of the menu is naturally gluten-free, accommodating many dietary restrictions and preferences.Esquire Magazine and Eater both named Baobab Fare one of the Best New Restaurants in the country when it debuted in 2021, and the restaurant and its owners have been recognized multiple times by the James Beard Awards as semifinalists. In 2024, co-owners Hamissi Mamba & Nadia Nijimbere were finalists in the Outstanding Restaurateur category.

name: Ladder 4 Wine Bar
description: Our menu changes with the seasons, and what we think looks good right now. We start with whatever we harvested from our garden this morning, or with whatever our farmers dropped off today. We're building relationships with the people who grow our food and make our wine - people who care as much about their craft as we do about ours.  We take our wine and our food seriously. Everything else? It’s just dinner with friends.Since opening in 2022, we've been recognized as the Detroit Free Press Restaurant of the Year in 2023, one of Bon Appetit's Best New Restaurants of 2023 and in America's Best Restaurants of 2023 list by the New York Times.Reservations are for dinner service. If you're joining us for drinks, please walk in for bar seats. Our bar is always kept available for walk-ins, for full dinner service, or just drinks. We ask that your entire party arrive on time. If you are more than 15 minutes late for your reservation, we may release it to a waiting party.

name: Soby's
description: Soby’s New South Cuisine is an eclectic blend of contemporary continental cuisine infused with traditional Southern ingredients, transforming old favorites into something fresh and new. The restaurant is located in the heart of downtown Greenville, in a renovated cotton mill built in the 1800s. The atmosphere is warm and energetic and the staff is well-trained to provide a fantastic dining experience for all of our guests. In addition, the wine list offers more than 3,100 selections and an inventory of 15,500 selections. The restaurant is a Wine Spectator Grand Award winner.

name: The Lazy Goat
description: The Lazy Goat is a Mediterranean-themed restaurant with a menu featuring global influences from Spain, Morocco, Italy, France, Greece, Africa and the Middle East in a relaxed atmosphere where taking the time to "linger and share" is encouraged. The diverse menu was created to accommodate a variety of diner preferences with meat & cheese plates, small plates to “graze and nibble,” and main dishes. Guests are invited to take their time while "trying a little bit of this and a little bit of that" or a "whole lot of whatever they like."

name: CAMP
description: CAMP/kamp/ verb: To relax, chill, unwind, savor, reflect, enjoy, breatheStep through the doors of CAMP and you know you’re in good company. Here is a gathering place that captures the essence of downtown Greenville perfectly—cool, comfortable, lively—with food that is insanely delicious and flavorful.The whimsical, comfort food menu with its touch of global flair is crafted using only the freshest, seasonal ingredients sourced directly from local farms. Refreshing cocktails, a curated wine list, and a selection of regional beers pair perfectly to ensure a remarkable dining experience. Come unwind, chill with friends, and savor the moment at CAMP.

name: Hau Tree
description: Hau Tree, at Kaimana Beach Hotel, offers a beach-centric menu in a casual setting. Hau Tree serves daily beachside brunch, and culinary casual dinner offerings. Our open-air outdoor dining room features oceanfront views of Kaimana Beach under a canopy of hau trees.

name: The Pig and the Lady
description: From it’s humble beginnings as a pop-up restaurant, to a farmer"s market stand and now a successful brick-and-mortar eatery, The Pig and the Lady, led by twice-nominated James Beard semifinalist Chef Andrew Le (with help from his family including his mother, Mama Le), cook up what is essentially the family meal in the truest sense --  Exposing Honolulu diners a cross-section of cuisine that has Vietnamese origins, but is eclectic and daring.Chef Le and his team have developed a modern, fresh take on traditional Southeast Asian food, utilizing local products -- serving their famous banh mi and pho, along with new favorites. Le is the one of the few chefs cooking contemporary food built on Southeast Asian flavors that has garnered rave reviews -- a foundation built on delicious memories first introduced by his mother. They are open Monday through Saturday, serving lunch and dinner. You can also still find them at the Farmer’s Markets at Blaisdell, Kailua and KCC.

name: Kaimuki Shokudo
description: Kaimuki Shokudo is a cozy Japanese restaurant and izakaya in Kaimuki, Honolulu, serving authentic Japanese comfort food and specialty soba dishes, including fresh soba, tempura soba, sushi rolls, and Honey Toast.After 18 years at Shokudo on Kapiolani Boulevard, we bring the same essence and hospitality to Kaimuki, with lunch soba specials and a seafood & veggie-focused izakaya menu for dinner.Perfect for casual lunch, dinner, late-night dining, happy hour, and group meals, Kaimuki Shokudo also offers sake, beer, cocktails, and Japanese-inspired drinks for a fun and relaxed dining experience.Conveniently located near 11th Avenue in Kaimuki, Kaimuki Shokudo is your destination for authentic Japanese cuisine, izakaya favorites, and memorable meals with friends in Honolulu.

name: MW Restaurant
description: Discover the vibrant flavors of Hawaii at MW Restaurant, where renowned chefs Wade Ueoka and Michelle Karr-Ueoka showcase their passion for local cuisine. Now on the 2nd floor of the Velocity Honolulu Building, our new location offers a refined yet casual dining experience that celebrates Hawaii’s rich culinary heritage.Conveniently located across from the Blaisdell Center, use “Velocity Honolulu” or “888 Kapiolani Blvd” for accurate directions. Valet parking is available at the entrance from Kapiolani Blvd up the ramp; avoid parking in the MW Valet-only stall on the ground level as it is subject to towing.Join us to savor exquisite regional dishes and delectable desserts that have made MW Restaurant a cornerstone of Hawaii’s culinary scene. Visit https://mwrestaurant.com/ to place your order or learn more.

name: Senia
description: Senia is one of Honolulu’s favorite restaurants for simply good food. We offer an à la carte menu with delicious, casual food made with exceptional craft. Chef Anthony Rush trained in multiple three Michelin-starred restaurants and creates beautiful dishes with passion and skill, but he believes everyday food should be delicious and well-crafted, too, so Senia was created to be a casual, affordable, neighborhood restaurant. We celebrate quality ingredients, creativity, classic techniques, and thoughtful hospitality. We welcome you in for dinner to share a meal with friends and loved ones in a comfortable, beautiful space. If you are looking for a fine dining experience, we invite you to join us at our Chefs’ Counter for our award-winning Tasting Menu. With eight seats and one seating on Friday and Saturday nights, our Tasting Menu is exclusive and luxurious. It’s not all fancy on the weekends, though. We always offer casual dining and our à la carte menu in our main dining room.

name: Lineage Maui
description: Nestled in the Shops of Wailea offering a true "foodie" experience, Lineage delights guests from around the globe and locals alike. Reservation phone line opens at 2pm daily (808)879-8800.  Please include in your guest count: infants, toddlers and children.While we do our best to accommodate special requests, we cannot make any guarantees.  Maximum of 2 split checks per reservation.15-minute late grace period from reservation time, if you are more than 15 minutes late, your reservation will be cancelled.Credit card is required to hold reservations of 7 or more.Cancellation Policy: $25 pp fee for cancellation or decrease in guest count within 24 hrs of reservation.Walk-ins: We welcome walk-ins if and when tables are availableOpen Tuesday through Saturday from 5 PM to 9 PM.  Closed Sunday & Mondays.  Mahalo!

name: ChopnBlok
description: Created by 2024 James Beard Emerging Chef Nominee, Ope Amosu, ChòpnBlọk is a nationally acclaimed restaurant that carefully unveils the West African culinary experience, serving fresh proteins, vegetables, grains, and beverages that are prepared under a West African flavor profile, packaged in a local, contemporary fashion.Freshness and integrity are embedded in our culture. We selectively source our ingredients and work with partners to procure natural, high-quality meats and vegetables.At its core, ChòpnBlọk is the cultural crossroads where local communities and West African culinary traditions intersect, to deliver a convenient, hospitable food experience with exquisite taste.

name: Rosie Cannonball
description: At Rosie Cannonball, we’re serving European comfort food in a lively atmosphere. The restaurant celebrates the origins of cooking through open, live fire and is centered around a wood-burning oven and grill. The menu draws inspiration from individual ingredients: local vegetables, pastas and pizzas, simple seafood and locally-sourced meat cooked over live fire. Master Sommelier June Rodil curated an expansive list of delicious wines with a strong Italian, Spanish and French focus from their favorite producers new and old, along with options from progressive New World producers.  The cocktail menu has strong Italian influences, mirroring the focus of the food. Chef de Cuisine: Melven Batocabe Sous Chef: Calvin MillerExecutive Pastry Chef: Micaela VictoriaGeneral Manager: Lauren AllenSommelier: Jordan RaabeGoodnight Hospitality Partners: Bailey & Peter McCarthy, Felipe Riccio, June Rodil

name: Maximo
description: Nestled in the heart of Houston’s West University neighborhood at 6119 Edloe Street, Maximo is a progressive Mexican restaurant from the Local Foods Group team that combines authenticity and adventure. Nearly everything is done in house under Executive Chef Adrian Torres with mentorship from Local Foods Group Creative & Culinary Director Seth Siegel-Gardner. Nixtamalized tortillas are just the start; corn kernels are boiled, soaked and ground into masa, imparting maximum flavor into a dedicated masa section on the menu featuring sopes, tetelas and even cornbread, along with a list of ever-changing taco options. Shareable plates, from crudos and salads to entrees and desserts, entice guests to explore. The restaurant is open Monday, Wednesday and Thursday from 4 - 9 p.m. for dinner service and Friday through Sunday from 11 a.m. - 9 p.m. for brunch and dinner service.

name: BCN Taste & Tradition
description: BCN Taste & Tradition, the name gives a nod to Barcelona and the meld of traditional and modern Spanish cuisine. Chef Luis Roger relocated from Girona, Spain - trained under Michelin Chef Ferran Adria at elBulli- and taught at the renowned culinary school Aula Gastronomica de l’Emporada. Located on a 1920’s era Victorian home in the trendy Montrose/Museum District (4210 Roseland St.). BCN Taste & Tradition will recreate an authentic Spanish style dining experience with unparalleled service. The menu showcases all the fantastic, cutting-edge haute cuisine that put Spain on the world stage. Credit card info required for reservations larger than 5 guests.  A $50/person fee will be charged if you are unable to cancel within 48 of your scheduled time or if your party decreases in size within 24 of your reservation. No fee will be assessed for transferring the reservation to a different name.

name: Agnes and Sherman
description: Named after chef's parents, Agnes and Sherman is a diner experience all about Asian American comfort food. Co-Owners Lisa Lee and Nick Wong's cozy new restaurant in the vibrant Houston Heights honor their unique third-culture Asian American roots, and everything that’s special about Houston. Inventive versions of familiar flavors are nestled alongside the golden rules of inclusion and belonging, as it’s now more important than ever to convey the values that have always been true: taking care of others is an act of love, and everyone is welcome here.We hold tables up to 15 min and we kindly ask for complete parties before seating. After 15 min we'll do our best to seat you but your reservation is not guaranteed. To honor your seating time, placing your whole order at the same time is highly recommended as dishes are meant to be shared family-style. A 22% service charge will be added to parties of 6+ and $5/person fee will apply for outside desserts served in-house.

name: Brasserie 19
description: Brasserie 19, located in Houston’s 77019 neighborhood, offers a modern amalgamation of French brasserie fare and well-known American influences. The menu utilizes the freshest seasonal ingredients in thoughtfully prepared dishes. Brasserie 19 boasts an expansive, carefully developed wine list. Brasserie 19's sophisticated yet unpretentious interiors are bathed in deep blues, cool greys, and whites, with antiqued woods and white marble used throughout. Blue velvet banquettes are paired with woven chairs and complemented by white linen-covered tables to create a casually elegant setting for lunch, dinner or weekend brunch. Brasserie 19 serves lunch and dinner 7 days a week, as well as brunch on Saturdays and Sundays.

name: Kiran's
description: James Beard semi-finalist for Best Chef Texas and Top Chef Season 19 judge Kiran Verma, considered the ‘godmother of Indian fine dining,’ has created world-class north Indian cuisine at her eponymous restaurant. Come and enjoy good food and great service in a fabulous environment.Signature dishes include rack of lamb with aloo methi, Chilean sea bass with mango chutney, chicken tikka masala and a vegetarian thali. Specialty cocktails range from the Jahangir Lassi (house-infused pineapple rum, passion fruit, mango lassi, pistachio) to a Chai Old Fashioned to a Cardamom Basil Gin & Tonic.The restaurant serves Afternoon Tea every Friday, Saturday and Sunday - a four-course Indian and English tea experience. Chef Kiran has created her own chai blend.

name: Turner's Cut
description: Turner’s Cut is Houston’s newest playground for the elite, mixing the sophistication and luxury of the Gilded Age, the indulgence and free-flowing excess of the Golden Era, spiced with the risqué, exclusive, and clandestine aura of prohibition. Featuring the most exquisite and rare cuts of meat from America and Japan, white-glove service, live bandstand music Friday and Saturday evenings, and antique design elements, Turner’s Cut is the most opulent dining experience in the city of Houston.

name: March
description: In the heart of Houston’s Montrose neighborhood, MARCH is an intimate Mediterranean restaurant from Chef Felipe Riccio. Serving a course tasting menu, we study the gastronomy, history, and cultures of the region to answer the question:“What is Mediterranean cuisine?”After many years of personal and professional exploration of the Mediterranean, Felipe has brought this spirit of exploration back to his hometown.The word ‘MARCH’ signifies an area of land on the border between two territories; a frontier between realms. The menu is the team’s love letter to the Mediterranean where we will explore the unique, layered evolution of its many connected cuisines, with beverage pairings/selections artfully curated by partner and Master Sommelier, June Rodil.Chef de Cuisine: Chris DaviesExecutive Pastry Chef: Micaela VictoriaGeneral Manager: Gabriel FontenierWine Director: Anthony SalazarGoodnight Hospitality Partners: Bailey & Peter McCarthy, Felipe Riccio, June Rodil

name: Októ
description: Welcome to Októ, where Mediterranean flavors and vibrant ambiance converge. Named after the Greek word for "eight," Októ offers an upbeat atmosphere with a jazzy bar featuring exclusive spirits. Our menu, crafted by SOf chef Yotam Dolev showcases innovative, nutritious dishes inspired by Greece, Spain, and the Levant.Guests can enjoy a seasonal selection of approximately a dozen shareable plates, reflecting the rich culinary traditions of the Mediterranean. Complementing the food, our bar serves a curated wine list and a range of classic and creative craft cocktails. Októ is designed for communal dining, making each visit a memorable celebration of Mediterranean cuisine and culture.

name: Lee's
description: Lee's celebrates all things Italian, with an amaro, Aperol, and Campari-focused cocktail menu full of bright spritzes. Enjoy irreverent snacks like mozzarella sticks, fried calamari, mushroom pizza, and a chicken parm sandwich in a moody, lounge-inspired atmosphere. Join us Wednesday night for pasta specials and Thursday for our Steak Night!Please note, Lee's is a 21+ establishment, physical photo ID is required for all guests.Named one of 2025's Best Bars in America (Bon Appetit)Nominated for 2026 Best New Bar (James Beard Foundation)

name: Street To Kitchen
description: Enjoy unapologetically authentic Thai food in the East End. All food is made from scratch by native Thai chef and James Beard Award winner for Best Chef: Texas Benchawan Jabthong Painter.Chef Benchawan and her husband—who also spend time in their Bangkok home each year—have painstakingly built out a restaurant that would fit into Bangkok’s hip Thong Lor neighborhood, but they have located it in Houston. Expect trendy Thai vibes, music, strong cocktails and an adventurous wine list to accompany Chef Benchawan’s acclaimed dishes.When booking your reservation, all children—regardless of age—must be included in the head count. The largest party Street to Kitchen can accommodate is 6 people. If more than 6, you can arrange a private Thai tasting menu, including a minimum spend. Please contact us via email at eatthai@streettokitchen.org to schedule.

name: Midtown Table
description: Welcome to Midtown Table, where the heart of the kitchen meets the soul of the community. As a proud Medure Brothers Concept with a scratch kitchen, we bring a fresh, artisan-inspired approach to the Town Center dining scene. Our menu is a celebration of simple, high-quality ingredients, featuring hand-crafted pastas, house-baked breads and unique signature dishes.Whether you're looking for the Best Outdoor Dining Jacksonville has to offer on our expansive patio or a cozy corner in our modern dining room, Midtown Table provides the perfect backdrop for any occasion. From a feast with the family to a refined evening of craft cocktails and conversation, our space is designed for connection. Experience the warmth of a true neighborhood kitchen where every meal is prepared with passion and served with genuine hospitality. Join us at Midtown Table—Jacksonville's premier destination for inspired, scratch-made cuisine in an inviting, upscale-casual atmosphere.

name: Rue Saint-Marc
description: Rue Saint-Marc is a modern restaurant serving French inspired cuisine in the San Marco neighborhood. We have an extensive cocktail program and a unique wine and craft beer list. The ambiance is stylish while still being quite comfortable. Seating includes tufted leather booths, handmade wooden tables, a beautiful bar-height communal table, and a covered outdoor patio. An open kitchen allows guests to view the chefs in action.

name: Matthew's Restaurant
description: Enjoy a dining experience that enlivens the senses at Matthew’s Restaurant. Located in the historic San Marco district, Chef Matthew Medure’s flagship destination has defined Fine Dining Jacksonville for almost three decades. Our open kitchen delivers a sophisticated Upscale French and Globally inspired experience, where classic techniques meet innovative global influences and the freshest seasonal ingredients.Whether you are joining us for a celebratory Jacksonville Anniversary Dinner or exploring our world-class Chef-Driven Tasting Menu, every visit is a choreographed masterclass in hospitality. Our award-winning wine cellar and meticulously crafted plates offer a sanctuary for the discerning palate. Discover why Matthew’s remains a cornerstone of the Florida dining scene—a place where elegance is effortless and every meal is a celebrated occasion. Immerse yourself in a tradition of excellence and let us redefine your expectations of a truly great meal.

name: Room 39 - Midtown
description: Discover Room 39—where farm-sourced ingredients inspire every meal, from breakfast through dinner. Led by chef-owner Ted Habiger, our menus evolve daily with local producers’ freshest offerings, and our award-winning wine list is curated by expert sommeliers to perfectly complement each dish. For over 20 years, we’ve welcomed guests into a cozy space filled with art, travel, and the personal passions we love to share. Located in the heart of Kansas City’s 39th Street, Room 39 is dedicated to great food and wine enjoyed among good friends in a setting that feels like home.

name: Pierpont's at Union Station
description: Pierpont's at Union Station has been providing food and service as memorable as the historic setting since its inception in 1999. Named for railroad baron J.P. (Pierpont) Morgan, Pierpont's serves perfectly aged prime steaks and fresh seafood arriving daily. To complement your culinary selections, we maintain an extensive wine list featuring bottles from all over the globe, as well as boast a gorgeous 2-story bar equipped with a rolling library ladder.Our Private Dining Rooms-located in the Wine Cellar, along with the Pierpont's Belvedere Room on the 2nd floor overlooking Grand Hall, offer more intimate settings for groups of 6 to 60. They are perfect for business or entertaining guests and each room is individually decorated, offering an exceptional place to celebrate a special occasion, close the deal, or have a gathering with friends.

name: JJ's Restaurant
