description: Our James Beard Award(2025) nominated wine list and beverage program is the big draw, but our steaks and seafood are the unsung heroes of the menu. For over 40 years, we have been the go to spot for wine, but many know us as their favorite neighborhood. We also have an outstanding happy hour M-F 3-6pm. Sunday 4-6pm.

name: Nobu - Caesars Palace Las Vegas
description: Nobu at Caesars Palace is a destination for a diverse and refined dining experience. The menu highlights imported Asian and Japanese fish, premium seafood, sushi rolls, and rare delicacies from around the world. Designed to impress even the most sophisticated tastes, the selection features only the freshest, finest ingredients. After the main dining room closes, the exclusive lounge menu presents unique items not available at other Nobu locations. For sake lovers, Nobu offers three exceptional choices from Hokusetsu brewery on Sado Island, including a hand-crafted variety that’s made through a meticulous slow drip process

name: Hell's Kitchen - Caesars Palace Las Vegas
description: Hell’s Kitchen Las Vegas pulls themes from all of Gordon Ramsay’s ventures incorporating the Hell’s Kitchen show, his signature items, his fiery personality and his overall brand to deliver a unique new restaurant. In order to create the experience the outlet will focus on the famous Red and Blue kitchens, show experiences, and a prominent bar that begs you to join us for a drink.**For groups of 13 or more reservations and/or private dining experiences, our Restaurant Group Sales Team is fielding requests through our website at https://www.caesars.com/las-vegas/restaurants/group-dining/group-dining-form. The team will respond within 48 hours, however someone will typically reach out within normal business hours, same day.**

name: Esther’s Kitchen
description: Seasonal Italian Restaurant.  Focused on Handmade pasta, bread and pizza.  We get all of our products as locally sourced as possible.

name: Top of the World Steakhouse - The STRAT Hotel, Casino & Tower
description: Located more than 800 feet above Las Vegas at The STRAT Hotel, Casino & Tower, the award-winning Top of the World offers a dining experience like no other. This Las Vegas fine dining restaurant features culinary delights served in an elegant atmosphere with unparalleled views of the city as the restaurant revolves 360 degrees every 80 minutes.

name: Vanderpump à Paris
description: Must be 21 years of age or older to enter this venue.Restaurateur and reality star Lisa Vanderpump brings vision to life with her newest Las Vegas creation.  Designed by artist Nick Alain, you will be set in a backdrop of the City of Light while dining on a variety of French Mediterranean style fare, and imbibing on whimsical cocktails that pay homage to Lisa’s time spent in France.Last Dinner Service reservation is at 10:15pm each evening.For Reservations of 10 or more people please contact VanderpumpParisFOH@caesars.com.

name: Harlo Steakhouse & Bar
description: Harlo is a classically elegant steakhouse developed by local restaurateur Jeffrey Fine. Conceptualized in the 60s, open today in Summerlin, Harlo provides an elevated ambiance for discerning clientele unlike anything else offered in Las Vegas.Chef Eduardo Saavedra brings his culinary excellence to Harlo, sourcing beef from the best farms around the globe. Leading the culinary team with passion and creativity, every dish served at Harlo is expertly curated and beautifully crafted. Discover a symphony of flavors with expertly prepared cuts and curated sides. Indulge in an elegant ambiance and attentive service. Experience culinary excellence at Harlo Steakhouse.

name: La Strega
description: LA STREGA TRATTORIA IS LOCATED IN BEAUTIFUL LAS VEGAS, NEVADA.La Strega is Las Vegas’ premiere Italian eatery nestled in the affluent community of Summerlin where James Beard nominated Chef Partner, Gina Marinelli ignites an authentic Italian cultural rejuvenation by inspiring exploration of the traditions of flavorful, fresh and innovative Italian cuisine with her modern, farm-to-table regionally-based dishes. Translating to “witch” in Italian, La Strega’s sophisticated yet playful atmosphere bewitchingly transports guests to Italy immediately upon arrival. Greens, clays, and earth tones make up its color palette, while full moon-shaped bulbs on brass chandeliers illuminate the space. The restaurant’s artistic centerpiece is a one‐of‐a‐kind mural that spans multiple walls throughout the restaurant, telling the story of the witches of Benevento which inspired the genesis of La Strega, a tale best told by the head Strega herself, Chef Gina Marinelli. We invite you into our home!!!

name: REGARDS
description: Bringing some LA vibes to the northeast.  REGARDS draws inspiration from the diverse food culture of Los Angeles.  We highlight flavor profiles from Japan and Mexico with the freshest of Maine produce and products.  The menu at REGARDS has bright, zippy ceviches and crudos, rich tamales, plump Argentinian shrimp tacos, along with mezcal cured hamachi collars, crab rice and grilled cabbage with caesar. Clarified lime margaritas with eucalyptus or our gin and sotol martinis with hibiscus pair with our adventurous menu.  We pour natural wine and have a large selection of curated mezcals and tequilas.A place for us and for you.  Might not make sense, but it sure is delicious.  Across the street from Reny's.  Nearby Maine College of Art, Portland Stage Company, State Theatre, Empire Comedy Club and Blue Jazz Club.  **The 50 Best New Restaurants of 2022, Bon Appetit****Plate Magazine Chef's To Watch, 2023****Star Chef New England, 2023**

name: Benny's
description: Benny's is a Philly-inspired, Italian-American restaurant in Portland's Arts District, from the team behind Ramona's Sandwich Shop. Enjoy a signature cocktail at our 14-seat bar, or settle down at a table for a night of classic dishes such as Meatballs, Linguine and Clams, Chicken Parmesan, or Pretzel Garlic Knots. Younger guests can enjoy a Shirley Temple while snacking on Mozzarella Sticks and a bowl of Spaghetti. Our dining room is fit for date nights and celebratory evenings. Benny’s is all about balance—nostalgic, comforting dishes made with the best ingredients and some modern twists for creative fun.

name: Mr. Tuna
description: A casual sushi joint in Portland, ME specializing in sustainable, local seafood. Mr. Tuna includes a poppy, pastel-shaded 30 seat sushi bar and seasonal outdoor seating. Chef Jordan Rubin is hyper focused on quality, earning accolades including a James Beard Nominee for Best Chef: Northeast and Food & Wine Best New Chef in 2025. Mr. Tuna was named #6 Best Restaurant in the Country by Food & Wine and was honored with the Innovation Award from Gulf of Maine Research Institute for its commitment to responsible sourcing. The menu is built around super-fresh, seasonal seafood sourced straight from the Gulf of Maine, as the restaurant is just a short walk up from Portland's waterfront. Try the Tuna Tasting, a four-cut journey through local bluefin, or the Tuna de Tigre, a bright, ceviche-style blend of Peruvian and Thai flavors. Don't skip the classics- the hand rolls are what brought Mr. Tuna to fame in the early food cart days. Save room for a hot pressed ice cream Toastie for dessert!

name: Nami Nori - Design District
description: Nami Nori is a casual, award. winning, temaki bar specializing in open-style sushi hand rolls. Our menu features signature temaki, including crunchy varieties, as well as takes on the classics and a special vegan section. The open-style temaki allows guests to enjoy a wide variety of flavors, in each fun-to-eat, visually tempting bite.We also serve Japanese-inspired snacks and appetizers, along with a selection of beer, sake and wine, available on tap and by the bottle. Nami Nori is a casual yet elegant temaki bar offering guests a unique sushi dining experience in Williamsburg. Coming from the rarified Japanese restaurants in which they trained, partners Taka Sakaeda, Jihan Lee and Lisa Limb envisioned Nami Nori to be a more inclusive place that balances accessibility with a dedication to quality and creativity with a respect for traditional techniques.Nami Nori is a place to share with friends, old and new.

name: Delilah - Miami
description: Delilah Miami is a modern-day supper club that brings a vibrant flair to the heart of Brickell. This vintage-inspired sanctuary immerses guests in Miami’s sultry spirit set aglow by lavish chandeliers that cast a warm glow upon its plush interior. Within this haven, guests are enveloped in nostalgia by the gentle crackling of the fireplace and the embrace of overstuffed armchairs, elevating comfort while indulging on exquisite American fare.Dining elevates to dancing as a troupe of female entertainers move to the sounds of a live jazz band. Eating, drinking, and dancing blur while guests relish in lounge-style bottle service deep into the rhythm of the night…oh yeah.

name: L’Atelier de Joël Robuchon - Miami
description: The first and only two Michelin star restaurant in Miami, and the only two Michelin star Joël Robuchon restaurant in the U.S., L'Atelier Miami of The Bastion Collection is the incarnation of the late Chef Joël Robuchon’s groundbreaking concept, staying true to the original Parisian roots while drawing inspiration from the surrounding city and location in the Miami Design District.L'Atelier’s name refers to a craftsman’s workshop, emphasizing Chef Robuchon’s hallmark of elevating the simplest of dishes into something extraordinary using pristine ingredients, immaculate technique and abundant creativity. The chef’s counter seating faces directly into the open kitchen, allowing for a dynamic interaction between the chefs and diners, and view into the creative process.

name: Luca Osteria
description: Luca Osteria brings the essence of Italy to Coral Gables, Miami. Led by Chopped champion Chef Giorgio Rapicavoli, the restaurant offers a memorable dining experience with a menu that blends classic Italian dishes and innovative creations, all crafted from fresh, seasonal ingredients. Start with the Tuna Caprese or indulge in the decadent Potato Puffs with egg yolk and truffle cream. For the main course, savor the hearty Pappardelle with Wild Boar Ragu or the light Branzino al Limone. The inviting atmosphere features a contemporary design with natural wood accents, and outdoor seating along Giralda Avenue creates a vibrant setting for dining under the Miami sky. Whether for a romantic dinner, a special celebration, or simply a delicious Italian meal, Luca Osteria is the ideal destination for experiencing exceptional Italian cuisine in a sophisticated and welcoming environment.

name: Los Félix Miami
description: At Los Félix, we celebrate the vibrant spirit of Mexican cuisine through the ancestral wisdom of Milpa farming—a sacred tradition that honors the connection between land and self. Our kitchen shines with native ingredients like heirloom corn, cacao, chiles, and squash, all brought to life in our in-house Molino, where maíz is cooked and ground daily for tortillas made from scratch.Helmed by Chef Sebastián Vargas, whose culinary journey bridges indigenous technique with modern artistry, Los Félix has earned both a MICHELIN Star for excellence and a MICHELIN Green Star for sustainability. Whether you're joining us for a soulful brunch or an intimate dinner, expect a journey from Milpa to mesa—where every dish tells a story, and every bite carries intention.On weekends, the energy rises with vinyl-spun DJ sets that turn the room into a celebration of sound, flavor, and community. Come for the food, stay for the vibe!

name: Zitz Sum
description: Zitz Sum is a 30 seat seasonal restaurant located inside of an historic building in Coral Gables, Florida. Our story, is a story of resilience, passion & commitment, that is translated in a plate of food, excellent service and unapologetic gracious hospitality.  Our current menu is an autobiographical journey reflecting Chef’s experiences and our affinity for Japanese ingredients and technique. We currently offer A la carte menu that changes with the seasons, and a Seven Course Tasting Menu that changes weekly. Our tasting menu consists of playful snacks and bites to get the night going, and an array of our signature and favorite current dishes. Part of the tasting menu is presented in a family style format conducive for sharing and celebrating. Zitz Sum is recipient of a 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025 & 2026 Michelin Guide Bib Gourmand Award, a 2022 James Beard Foundation Semifinalist for Best New Restaurant and a New York Times 2022 Best 50 Restaurants in America.

name: Krüs Kitchen
description: They say the kitchen is the heart of every home—and at the heart of Coconut Grove, Krüs Kitchen pulses with the vision of Chef Sebastián Vargas. With a résumé that spans MICHELIN-starred icons like Osteria Francescana, Eleven Madison Park, and Fäviken Magasinet, Chef Vargas brings a globally refined, culturally rooted perspective to every plate.At Krüs, the menu evolves with the seasons, spotlighting consciously sourced ingredients and deep relationships with local farmers and purveyors. The result is more than a meal—it’s a high-vibe invitation to connect, nourish, and become part of a community grounded in flavor and integrity.Join us for a vibrant lunch of fresh salads, hearty sandwiches, and homemade market treats. By evening, our rotating dinner menu channels Miami’s energy with peak-season ingredients and bold, expressive dishes. Every visit is a chance to experience the rhythm of Krüs—where heritage meets innovation, and every bite tells a story.

name: Eating House
description:
Eating House is a culinary gem in Coral Gables, where Chef Giorgio Rapicavoli showcases his creativity through modern American cuisine. The menu features an exciting array of small plates and entrees that push flavor boundaries. Enjoy dishes like heirloom tomatoes with fish sauce, lime, and coconut shavings, or crispy cauliflower with crema, cotija, and corn. Don’t miss the famous Cap'n Crunch pancakes, topped with a rich condensed milk syrup. 

The inviting ambiance features black tables, a plant wall, and striking graffiti art, all centered around an open kitchen where you can see the culinary magic unfold. Whether you're a local craving upscale comfort food or a visitor exploring Miami's vibrant dining scene, Eating House offers a memorable experience with innovative dishes and exceptional service. Come and discover why Eating House is a must-visit destination for food lovers.

name: Phuc Yea
description: Phuc Yea offers a selection of shareable plates that capture the soulfulness and passion behind family-rooted recipes, the restaurant is inspired by Aniece Meinhold's Vietnamese heritage and Cesar Zapata's Third Coast culinary roots. Under one roof, guests are invited to marvel at Biscayne's blossoming Mimo District; embrace 90's hip-hop, street-cool music; and enjoy Cesar's version of Viet- Cajun cuisine - a lively mash up of fresh and colorful Vietnamese ingredients pumped up with spicy, bold Cajun flavors. This grown up version of Phuc Yea is largely seafood driven.Brunch features a dim sum style menu that highlights classic American brunch preparations. It is at brunch, Chef Zapata will feature his Gulf and Southern influences more prominently while still integrating Vietnamese breakfast tradition.

name: Ghee - Downtown Dadeland
description: A pursuit for knowledge was the stimulus and continues to be the driving force behind Chef Niven’s career.  Ghee means PURE and has a symbolic meaning both spiritually and in the kitchen of an Indian home. The restaurant encompass traditional Indian home cooking, ingredient driven using local artisan purveyors. Chef Niven highlights the experience of dining at Ghee: "I want to my guests to walk into Ghee with the sense of entering my home. I want them to feel at home. I want them to smell the aromas and spices and get more engaged in their meal. One of the things that I always tell my cooks is, ask questions, be inquisitive if you don’t know. I want the same for my guests."

name: The Den
description: Azabu Miami Beach, houses various experiences under one roof; tied together with the spirit of Omatenashi, the essence of Japanese hospitality. Beyond Azabu lies The Den, an exclusive hidden omakase room specializing in the art of Edomae Sushi helmed by Master Sushi Chefs. Located inside The Stanton Hotel, The Den at Azabu Miami Beach elevates the local culinary scene with authentic Japanese cuisine and transportive experiences. The Den serves Edomae Tokyo style sushi. The Den is a 18 seat counter, omakase style sushi restaurant, where Sushi chefs will guide guests through an authentic omakase experience. Most ingredients will be flown in directly from the Toyosu fish market in Tokyo.  All guest must consume raw fish. Chef's do their best to accommodate dietary restrictions if informed in advance. The Den has 3 different menu offerings for guest to select upon booking. Strict 15 minutes grace period and 24 hour cancellation with no acceptions. Please see cancellation policy.

name: Stage Kitchen & Bar - Boca
description: Flavor forward cuisine crafted through intuition... STAGE (/STAAHJ/) We welcome you to Stage—an integrated, small-plates experience from James Beard Foundation Award-nominee Chef Pushkar Marathe, inspired by diverse culinary influences from across the world. Discover innovative dishes interwoven with tradition, utilizing fresh and often local ingredients that come together in vital, new and flavorful ways.

name: Taki Omakase - Delray
description: Taki Omakase is Delray Beach’s premier Japanese Omakase restaurant. We offer a curated 18-course chef’s choice menu - including traditional Japanese nigiri and cutting-edge, innovative seafood & wagyu dishes. We have 3 nightly seatings Friday & Saturday at 5:00, 7:00, & 9:00 pm. Tuesday-Thursday & Sunday we offer 2 seatings at 6:00 & 8:00 pm. Lunch seating available on Friday, Saturday and Sunday's at 12:30 pm.  I understand Dinner Omakase is $180 per person  + tax & gratuity. Lunch Omakase is $100  per person  + tax & gratuity. A deposit of half the Omakase ($100 at dinner $50 at lunch)  is required to book and will be applied toward the bill. ***Any changes or cancellations occur WITHIN the 24 hour period of your reservation day and  time will forfeit  your deposit/refund. ***All guests must consume raw fish.  We cannot accommodate vegan or vegetarian restrictions.

name: DanDan Milwaukee
description: DanDan Milwaukee has a Chinese soul with a Midwestern sensibility. Flavorful, respectful, playful and seasonal, it’s an homage to the foods of our childhood.

name: EsterEv
description: Located in Milwaukee's Bay View neighborhood, EsterEv's offerings evoke globally inspired nostalgic flavors from grandma's kitchen. Presented by James Beard Best Chef Midwest nominees Dan Jacobs and Dan Van Rite. Cancellation Policy: If you need to cancel or alter your reservation for any reason, we require a 24-hour notice. If cancelations or alterations are made within the 24 hours, a $40 charge, per person cancelled, will be made to the credit card on file. This includes reduction in party size without notice, and is non-refundable.

name: Manny's Steakhouse
description: Masculine, bright, accented with wood & leather, Manny's evokes the great steakhouses of New York & Chicago - & eclipses them in critical & popular acclaim. Rated Top Five in the U.S. by the Zagat Guide, named one of America's top Steakhouses by the Knife & Fork Club, Men's Journal & the Independent Cattleman's Association, & a perennial winner of newspaper & magazine reader polls, Manny's commands a fanatical local following & a national reputation. Wrote the New York Times: "There's good news in Minneapolis for those who still share the secret pleasures of red meat, well-cut & seriously served." In addition to USDA-certified, dry- aged, center-cut beef, Manny's offers chops, chicken, fresh seafood & a Wine Spectator Award-winning wine list.

name: P.S. Steak
description: As the name suggests, P.S. Steak was established to continue the story of the historic space that was once famed La Belle Vie. As Chef Mike DeCamp spent 10 years at the former French restaurant, this will be his homecoming and opportunity to write his own chapter for the space. P.S. Steak disrupts the perceptions of a typical steakhouse while honoring the legacy of one of the great culinary foundations in the Twin Cities. The restaurant features an elevated yet accessible seasonal menu, along with a variety of cuts of meat, dry aged in a display case.The lounge has been given a modern makeover with chic furniture and marble table tops. A selection of living floor plants divides tables to create intimate seating. Bringing warmth to the room, the bar and adjacent fireplace keeps guests cozied up to a menu of progressive cocktails.

name: Baldamar
description: If you're going to take your name from a badass like the patron saint of blacksmiths, you better be able to back it up. Baldamar marks the turning point where we usher in the new standard for style, service and steak. It means the menu’s made with old-world craft at its core and modern day meat eaters in mind — with flavors forged in fire and folded into pure primal perfection. It’s where every steak is aged 40 days and 40 nights for a taste of biblical proportion, and where crazy-fresh seafood means it was caught during your morning commute. At the bar sits every spirit imaginable, and someone who isn’t afraid to use them. This is more than a new steakhouse. It’s a line in the sand. It’s not just where you celebrate special occasions. It’s where you create them.*A 20% Service Fee is added to all groups of 6 or more.

name: Blackbird Modern Asian - Naples
description: Walk-ins are always welcome! Bar and Lounge are first-come, first-serve. If you would like to make a reservation for a party of 7 or more, please contact us at hello@blackbirdmodernasian.com or (239) 944-2473About Us:    *  Voted Top 100 Independent Restaurants in America — Restaurant Business Magazine    *  Voted number 1 restaurant in Palm Beach County— Yelp Broward/Palm Beach

name: Tigress Rooftop Restaurant & Bar
description: Tigress Cantonese Chophouse perches atop The Perry Hotel, Naples, on the seventh-floor rooftop. Chef Dale Talde's innovative menu blends Cantonese flavors with American chophouse classics. Indulge in Dry-Aged Crown of Duck, Black Angus Rib-Eye with soy black garlic butter, and Blue Crab Fried Rice. Savor panoramic views of the River and Gulf while enjoying a romantic dinner or celebrating a special occasion.Just steps away, Easy Tiger Rooftop Bar awaits, an open-air oasis designed by mixology legend Lynnette Marrero. Her inventive cocktails, like the Tigress-Tini and Hong Kong Old-Fashioned, complement Tigress' diverse menu perfectly.Whether you're savoring a delectable dish at Tigress or sipping a crafted cocktail at Easy Tiger, both venues offer an unforgettable experience. Immerse yourself in world-class cuisine and cocktails while enjoying breathtaking views.

name: Ocean Prime - Naples
description: Located on Naples' icon Fifth Avenue South, Ocean Prime is a nationally acclaimed, dynamic seafood & steakhouse from the award-winning Cameron Mitchell Restaurants. Ocean Prime delivers an elevated experience with exceptional ambiance, a chef-curated menu, signature handcrafted cocktails, Wine Spectator honored wine list and genuine hospitality. Reservations are recommended.Ocean Prime strives to provide an elegant, fine dining atmosphere. Our associates’ priority is to ensure your experience is memorable. We ask that our guests choose attire that is both polished and suited to our restaurant.THANK YOU FOR NOT WEARING: Clothing emitting excessive marijuana odor Excessively revealing clothing or exposed undergarments Clothing with offensive language or graphics Admittance to Ocean Prime is at the discretion of management who reserves the right to refuse service to patrons who are inappropriately dressed.

name: Oise Ristorante - Fort Myers
description: Oise is a Italian restaurant with Japanese inspirations, this concept is lead by Miami based and James Beard Award-nominated Chef Brad Kilgore! Blending the precision of Italian traditional dishes that we all know and love with a fun and delicious Japanese touch. Our sushi program compliments the menu along with a wonderful offering of grilled items from our Yakitori style grill station. Overall if you are looking for a new, delicious experience in downtown Ft. Myers we are the location for you. Signature dishes like the Truffle Sesame Carbonara and Chicken Parm katsu showcase our commitment exciting flavors and Chef Brads takes on Classics. The cocktail program is unlike any other in the area, hand made from scratch infusions and syrups to create a specialty list alongside classics cocktails will complete the wonderful Oise dining experience.

name: Pelato - Nashville
description: Pelato is the beloved Brooklyn-Italian, family-run restaurant offering a memorable dining experience with small sharable plates featuring cherished family recipes. A mural wall adorned with original photographs reflects the family's Brooklyn heritage.The menu celebrates authentic Italian flavors with a Brooklyn twist, featuring classic dishes like Potato Croquettes, Baked Clams Oreganata, and Chicken Parmigiana, along with house-made pastas.  Don't miss the classic desserts like rainbow cookies & zeppoles to complete your meal.Outdoor dining available year-round in our enclosed, covered, & heated patio.  Bar seating available!20% gratuity applied to groups of 6+. Split checks may be divided up to 4 credit cards at equal value. ​BYOB Corkage fee: $45 per 750mL (limit 2 bottles per table). Outside dessert fee: $5 per person. Due to high demand, tables are subject to a 2-hour limit. Kids menus not available, server will recommend kid friendly options. Bar guests must be 21+yo.

name: Locust Nashville
description: **August reservations will open Wednesday, July 1st at 10:00am** ** To provide the best possible experience for all guests, the party size confirmed at booking is considered final. Reductions in guest count upon arrival will result in forfeiture of deposit. If you'd like to increase your guest count, please reach out directly at info@locustnashville.com. Locust brings a chaotic elegance to Nashville's dining scene. Chef Trevor Moran and team carefully assemble an ever-changing menu highlighting seafood and local, seasonal products: think raw bar selection, whole grilled fish, a decadent crab omelette, a big steak, all rounded out with a somehow chewy and soft shaved ice. In November 2025, Locust was awarded a MICHELIN Star.Lunch: Friday - Sunday 12:00 PM - 3:00 PM (reservations required)Dinner: Friday - Sunday 6:00 PM - 9:00 PM (reservations required)*Please note that Locust is a CARD ONLY establishment, and does not accept cash payments at this time.

name: Yolan
description: Recently named one of OpenTable’s Top 100 Hotel Restaurants in America, Yolan is a true love letter to Italy—an authentic experience of Italian cuisine, wine, and hospitality in the heart of downtown Nashville. Located within The Joseph, the restaurant honors the heritage of the founding family and its namesake, Yolanda.Evenings feature house-made pastas, wood-fired meats, and seasonal dishes shaped by Italy’s diverse regions, with à la carte selections or the five-course Esperienza Yolan tasting.By day, Yolan offers breakfast, weekday lunch, and weekend brunch. Italian-inflected morning fare, signature pastries, and spritzes pair with a vibrant espresso bar, while lunch offers elegant, midday dishes crafted with the same regional inspiration and care.Daily Afternoon Aperitivo invites guests to enjoy cocktails and small plates as the day unwinds. From sunrise to evening’s end, Yolan reflects the soul of Italian dining—warm, refined, and beautifully composed.

name: Bastion
description: **August reservations will open Wednesday, July 1st at 12:00pm** Bastion is the 24-seat restaurant of James Beard Foundation Award Nominee Chef Josh Habiger.  It is located in a separate area within the casual cocktail bar of the same name, and it is a part of Nashville's Wedgewood Houston neighborhood.  In November 2025, Bastion was awarded a MICHELIN Star.Bastion's dining room features counter seating around the kitchen for parties of 2 as well as three tables for parties of 3 to 6.  Bastion's small team serves a weekly set menu of six dinner courses featuring many dishes with a wide variety of flavors, ingredients, and inspirations.  Friendly service guides you through the culinary experience, which also features wine pairings, creative cocktails, and a curated vinyl soundtrack.  Come early or stay late in the hidden Little Bar, which is a private offering for guests of the restaurant.

name: Bad Idea
description: Bad Idea is a wine bar and restaurant in East Nashville focused on Contemporary American cooking and seasonal, locally sourced ingredients. Founded by Alex Burch, recipient of the Michelin American South Sommelier Award 2025, the concept places wine at the center of the experience, with a dynamic, food-friendly list designed for exploration and balance.The culinary program is directed by David Breeden, whose approach emphasizes technique, restraint, and letting exceptional ingredients speak for themselves. The result is a refined yet approachable neighborhood destination for both casual evenings and special occasions. Bad Idea was also named a semifinalist for Best New Restaurant in the 2025 James Beard Awards.

name: Henrietta Red
description: Located in Nashville's Germantown neighborhood, Henrietta Red is an acclaimed oyster bar and restaurant. It boasts a welcoming barroom, spacious dining room, oyster bar, and a temperature-controlled, enclosed patio.Henrietta Red’s approach to dining centers on fresh, seasonal dishes intended for sharing, fostering an inviting atmosphere ideal for meals with familiar and new friends. Our menu highlights local TN produce and responsibly sourced seafood, offering an extensive variety of oysters from coast to coast. Henrietta Red was named one of America’s 50 Best New Restaurants by Bon Appétit, one of GQ’s Best New Restaurants in America, and a James Beard Foundation Awards Semifinalist for Best New Restaurant in 2018. Chef Julia has been recognized as one of the Best Young Chefs in America by Robb Report and as one of Food & Wine Magazine’s Best New Chefs in 2018. She was also a 2019 semifinalist and 2020 Nominee for Best Chef: Southeast by the James Beard Foundation Awards.

name: International Market - Nashville
description: International Market originally opened in 1975, introducing generations of Nashvillians their first taste of Thai cuisine. Now across the street from the original location, Arnold and Anna Myint continue the family tradition by day and serve an evolved chef-curated menu by night. Inspired by their heritage and southern roots, International Market serves authentic Thai flavors by means of local and regionally sustainable purveyors.International Market has earned the Thai Select Classic Award, a prestigious recognition from the Ministry of Commerce and Royal Thai Government, a first for Nashville. This award, akin to Michelin recognition, is prominent in major U.S. cities like Chicago and New York. Criteria include anonymous dining visits evaluating food authenticity, heritage, atmosphere, and hospitality. The restaurant upholds original owners Win and Patti Myint's culinary legacy, and showcases Chef Arnold's modern Thai cooking approach.

name: GW Fins
description: Experience the cuisine that has created Raving Fans of local diners & national & local food critics alike. Executive Chef Mike Nelson ensures the highest quality seafood by flying in products from around the globe & featuring only the best from local waters - both at their seasonal peak. This commitment to freshness & quality are some of the many reasons GW Fins prints its menu daily. We look forward to serving you soon so we may learn what you think.We hope our dress policy is comfortable for all. Coats & ties are not required. Slacks, dress jeans, & dress shorts are great. We require that gentlemen wear a collared shirt. T-shirts, cut off shorts, & athletic wear, including baseball caps, are not permitted. We ask gentlemen to remove their hats in the Dining Room.

name: Brennan's
description: Celebrating 80 years in 2026 • Wine Spectator Grand Award • James Beard Foundation Finalist • The New York Times Top 25 (New Orleans) & Top 50 (U.S.) • Condé Nast Traveler “Best Restaurants in the World.”Welcome to Brennan's, in the heart of the French Quarter! Chef Kris Padalino's menus marry classic Creole traditions with inspired, contemporary influences. Breakfast at Brennan’s is a spirited, indulgent way to begin your day! Bubbles at Brennan's Happy Hour features discounted bottles of premium Champagne. Flaming Bananas Foster, invented at Brennan's, is a timeless New Orleans tradition. Dine in one of 8 glamorous dining rooms, our famously lush courtyard, or our spectacular wine cellar.

name: Jewel of the South
description: Borrowing its name from an influential 1850s-vintage bar in the Central Business District, this pub-like tavern  (with courtyard) occupies an old brick Creole cottage in the French Quarter, where well-known local bartender Chris Hannah serves up cocktails both classic and contemporary. London-born chef Philip Whitmarsh runs the kitchen.Accolades include James Beard, World 50 Best, Art of Hospitality, Best Bar South (North America).

name: La Petite Grocery
description: Come dine in this beautifully restored Uptown restaurant on Magazine Street and enjoy James Beard Award Winning Chef Justin Devillier's contemporary Louisiana Cuisine.The La Petite Grocery name pays homage to the history of its home, a building that’s stood for more than a century. The original structure was built in the late 1800s and called the Central Tea, Coffee and Butter Depot. In 1908, a fire devastated the space and it was transformed into a full service grocery store. Later it was most notably a fine foods and butcher shop, VonderHaar’s Fine Foods until 1982. When La Petite Grocery opened in March of 2004, they similarly aspired to provide the neighborhood with exotic teas, locally roasted coffee, and fresh produce. By doing this, we hope to provide a dining experience that evokes nostalgia of old New Orleans. Reservations are preferred.

name: Muriel's Jackson Square
description: The Bistro at Muriel’s Jackson Square offers contemporary Creole cuisine with New Orleans classics like Seafood Gumbo, Turtle soup, Shrimp & Grits and Wood Grilled Pork Chop while the Courtyard Bar is the perfect place to drop by for drinks and appetizers.  The Séance Lounge is the place for a relaxed, comfortable meeting before or after dining.  Comprised of two areas, The Outer Seance and The Inner Séance, the Séance also tells the story of a mystic checkered past.  The Outer Séance addresses the New Orleans era of Storyville when Muriel's Jackson Square was one of the French Quarter's top bordellos!  The Inner Séance is the preferred domain of the resident ghost, Pierre Antoine Lepardi Jourdan; it's the perfect spot for an intimate conversation or a drink among its plush surroundings. The 170 foot wrap-around balcony is a splendid spot for enjoying our specialty cocktails or our extensive wine list featuring over 300 selections from around the world.

name: Restaurant August
description: August is a Contemporary Creole restaurant creating unique dishes with a focus on Louisiana ingredients and inspired by classical training. Located in a historic 19th century French-Creole building in New Orleans’ Central Business District, the main dining room caters to the most unique dining experience.  The interior is rich with original architectural details, gleaming hardwood floors, soaring columns, mahogany paneling and antique mirrors.

name: Tableau
description: For large parties, please call (504) 934-3463.Located in the heart of the French Quarter, on the corner of Jackson Square, Tableau is Dickie Brennan's latest production and features traditional Creole dishes with an updated approach. Enjoy courtyard dining, as well as balcony dining with some of the best views in the Quarter. Our beautiful balconies offer the best view of Jackson Square.

name: Shaya
description: Inspired by the many amazing meals with family and dear friends throughout the Middle East, Shaya has been a trailblazer in modern Levantine cuisine. Drawing inspiration from the diverse cuisines & cultures of the Middle East, Mediterranean, and across North Africa, Shaya has set the benchmark for contemporary Mediterranean cuisine by honoring the age-old culinary traditions of the people who invented hospitality as we know it. Our fluffy hand-crafted pita bread baked to order in our wood-fired oven with delectable vegetable-driven dips, salads, and spreads will transport you to an exotic place. Today we hope that our food and hospitality will help you create delicious memories with your friends and family using the very best of our local and sustainably harvested ingredients, prepared and served by people who truly care.

name: The Bower
description:
The Bower is a culinary gem in the heart of New Orleans, where Southern charm meets modern American cuisine. Experience an inviting ambiance with lush greenery and industrial accents, illuminated by candlelight for an intimate dining experience. 

Executive Chef Marcus Woodham has crafted a menu that showcases locally sourced ingredients and innovative techniques. Delight in small plates like Truffled Parmesan Bread and Whipped Feta, or enjoy house-made pastas that highlight the chef's skill.

The beverage program features expertly crafted cocktails and a curated selection of wines, ensuring the perfect pairing for your meal. 

Whether celebrating a special occasion or seeking a memorable night out, The Bower offers warm hospitality, exquisite cuisine, and a captivating atmosphere that promises an unforgettable evening.

name: San Lorenzo at Hotel Saint Vincent
description: Our signature, all-day restaurant (named for the patron saint of cooks) is an ode to seasonal Italian cooking with regional New Orleans influences. The timeless design is grand, with lush modern touches and ample outdoor seating on the porch and courtyard.Menu highlights include seafood, grilled meats, and handmade pasta dishes like Dressed Oysters, Bresaola & Artichokes, Spring Pea Agnolotti, and Bistecca alla Fiorentina.Please find our full menu here: https://saintvincentnola.com/dining

name: Red Hook Tavern
description: Red Hook Tavern brings classic American tavern fare to the waterfront village in Brooklyn, where friends can share a bottle of wine, a few laughs, and a great meal. We hope from the moment you walk into this beautiful old space it makes you feel as special as it did the first time I walked in, almost five years ago. Cheers, Billy

name: Sailor
description: Sailor is a neighborhood bistro celebrating Chef April Bloomfield’s menu of elevated takes on familiar dishes. At the heart of the restaurants is it's deep wine list, which ranges from easy drinkers through classics and more esoteric selections. Tying it all together is the thoughtful service that Happy Cooking Hospitality has become known for.

name: San Sabino
description: San Sabino is a seafood focused restaurant from wife and husband team, chefs Angie Rito and Scott Tacinelli, in partnership with Quality Branded. After working in some of NYC’s top Italian restaurants and dreaming of opening their own special neighborhood spot, Angie and Scott opened Don Angie in 2018 to tremendous critical acclaim. They quickly became known for boldly unexpected dishes that challenge your perception of traditional Italian-American cooking.Six years later and just a few doors down from Don Angie, they've now opened their second restaurant, San Sabino. Named after Scott’s grandfather (from Sanza, Italy, where most people are named after the patron saint Sabino), San Sabino offers a playful spin on Italian dishes from Shrimp Parm to Pasta Alle Vongole, with bold twists influenced by coastal American cities where many Italians settled, from New Orleans to the Jersey Shore.

name: Don Angie
description: Don Angie is an intimate restaurant serving up a modern ode to Italian-American cuisine by husband and wife chefs and co-owners Angela Rito and Scott Tacinelli, in partnership with Quality Branded. Located in the heart of New York City’s West Village, the restaurant’s menu reimagines comforting dishes from Angie and Scott’s childhoods, with distinctive twists, often drawing inspiration from their collective travels.  Shortly after opening, Don Angie earned a 2-star critic’s pick review from the New York Times and named one of Esquire’s Best New Restaurants in America in 2018. Scott and Angie earned nominations from the James Beard Foundation in 2019, 2020 2022, and 2025. In May 2021, Don Angie was awarded a Michelin star.

name: Manhatta
description: Manhatta is a modern New York restaurant in the heart of downtown, shaped by the pace and energy of the city. The kitchen is helmed by Executive Chef Michele Brogioni, whose ever-evolving menu draws on traditional techniques and global influences. We offer a prix fixe lunch and multi-course dinner in the dining room, each paired with an award-winning wine list. In the bar, an à la carte menu is served daily.

name: Razza - Jersey City
description: Razza is an intimate pizzeria  in Jersey City, NJ centered around wood-fired pizzas made with locally sourced ingredients. Chef and owner Dan Richer, a six-time James Beard Award–nominated chef, is known for challenging conventional pizza-making practices with the goal of elevating the craft of pizza in America. The restaurant offers seasonally driven small plates, housemade bread and butter and a thoughtfully curated bar program. The only pizzeria to receive a three-star review from The New York Times, Razza was named Best Pizza in North America by 50 Top Pizza in 2019. The restaurant and Chef Dan Richer have been featured on Somebody Feed Phil, CBS Saturday morning and CNN’s Breaking Bread. Reservations are available 28 days in advance at noon.

name: Union Square Cafe
description: Founded in 1985, Union Square Cafe pioneered a new way of dining, drawing inspiration from the Union Square Greenmarket and showcasing the best local ingredients in a casual and contemporary dining room characterized by warmth and hospitality. Having trained and mentored scores of restaurant professionals for over three decades, Union Square Cafe is the birthplace of a generation of renowned restaurants, chefs, and restaurateurs.The restaurant has won five James Beard Awards and ranked in first place on Zagat Survey’s Most Popular Restaurant list an unprecedented nine times.

name: SAPPEISAN
description: SAPPE is a trip to explore something unique, authentic and ethereal. Our guest will be indulged and pampered through all their five perceptions; visual, auditory, olfactory, haptic and gustatory. SAPPE is the extraordinary place where people come to rejoice and revel. The train market and street food style are what SAPPE aim to offer. The vibe of bustling ambience of train station, the food stalls that offer street bites ‘ปิ้งย่าง (Ping-Yang)’ which is variety of grilled goodies on bamboo skewers and BBQ, as well as the bold flavor of E-San style cuisine are what SAPPE is looking to introduce.

name: Felina Steak- Jersey City
description: Located directly on the Hudson River, Felina Steak is a sleek, modern shrine to Italian-inspired steaks, chops, and seafood. The entrance showcases a dry-aging room with prime cuts and a two-tiered live lobster tank. Inside, an open kitchen adds energy to the intimate dining room, where guests enjoy views of the Manhattan skyline and the Statue of Liberty while watching the culinary team work with focus and precision. The menu is globally inspired yet rooted in bold, flavor-driven dishes that highlight high-quality ingredients and thoughtful techniques. Guests can sip expertly crafted cocktails at our bar, Nove, or explore our curated wine list with help from our sommelier. Whether you're here for a night out or a special occasion, Felina Steak delivers a unique and elevated experience. Outdoor dining is now available on a first come, first served basis, weather permitting. Be sure to visit the Stork Club Rooftop after dinner for more cocktails and breathtaking views.

name: Ci Siamo
description: Ci Siamo translates to "here we are" or "we've finally arrived." It's the clinking of glasses after a long week, the rush of arriving at a new destination, the relief of a familiar smile — it's an exhalation. Celebrating the beauty of simplicity, Chef Hillary Sterling centers her Italian-inflected menu around live-fire cooking and seasonal ingredients, bridging the traditional with the contemporary in a transportive yet familiar space.

name: Una Pizza Napoletana
description: Reservations open 14 days in advance at 9 AM ET.Chef/Owner Anthony Mangieri has been crafting naturally-leavened, wood-fired pizzas with meticulous attention to detail since 1996. A pioneer of Neapolitan pizza in the U.S., Anthony still makes every ball of dough by hand and is at Una making pizza every night. A few starters and desserts complement the pizzas, along with wines from Campania and Champagne. Everything is made fresh daily until sold out. The choice to have only one location allows for an obsessive focus on every detail.Una has been awarded #1 pizzeria in the world by 50 Top Pizza for 2022, 2024, and 2025, a MICHELIN Bib Gourmand, and recognized in media outlets including the TODAY Show, CBS Mornings, and Esquire. Open Thursday, Friday and Saturday. Reservations from 5:00 - 9:30 for parties of up to four. Reservations that have been traded or purchased through third party platforms will not be honored.

name: Maddy Rose at Liberty House
description: Where bold flavors meet unbeatable waterfront city views. Whether you’re diving into our perfectly seared steaks, ultra-fresh seafood, or signature craft cocktails, every bite (and sip) is a vibe. We offer a menu that speaks to every palate.You may remember us as Liberty House, and rest assured, we are still the same mouthwatering food and cocktails, unmatched level of hospitality, and unbeatable skyline waterfront views, we just decided to rename the restaurant after our daughter, Maddy Rose.Located on the Jersey City waterfront, Maddy Rose Restaurant offers a dining experience like no other. With panoramic views of the New York City skyline, indulge in expertly prepared steaks, fresh seafood, and elevated yet approachable cuisine that brings bold flavors to every plate. Whether you're joining us for a romantic dinner, a lively night out, or cocktails by the water, our restaurant is designed to impress.

name: Kabawa
description: Kabawa, a deeply personal restaurant from Chef Paul Carmichael and Momofuku, offers a nuanced and distinct perspective on Caribbean cuisine. Built around a set menu with plenty of choices, it’s a place to take your time, enjoy a meal, and discover something new each time you visit.Kabawa’s menu is a three-course, $150 prix fixe experience, exclusive of beverage, tax, and gratuity.  The restaurant is divided between kitchen counter seating, an immersive experience with direct chef service, or more secluded table seating.Structured to showcase the depth and diversity of Caribbean cuisine, our ingredients are thoughtfully procured, fusing local produce from neighborhoods right here in New York with specialty items sourced directly from the Caribbean. Every touch point — from the custom mosaics to the ingredient sourcing to the instrumental music — is intentional, tapping into Chef Paul’s upbringing in Barbados to reflect the vibrant, welcoming spirit of a Caribbean home.

name: Hawksmoor NYC
description: Located in the heart of Gramercy in the restored Assembly Hall of the landmarked United Charities Building, Hawksmoor is a steak restaurant that celebrates all-natural, pasture-reared, and traditionally dry-aged beef. Our beef is seasoned simply with sea salt and cooked over live-fire charcoal. Our seafood is locally and sustainably sourced from northeastern waters and we use small-farm produce whenever possible. On Sunday afternoons Hawksmoor keeps with the tried-and-true British tradition and features a Sunday Roast. Completing our offerings are award-winning cocktails, including New York City's coldest martini, and a carefully curated wine selection. The restaurant houses 150 in the dining room and 35 in the bar area. There are also two private dining rooms that can hold 12 people and serve exclusive, family-style menus.

name: Gjelina - New York
description: Gjelina is a neighborhood restaurant that serves locally-sourced produce,  hand-made pizzas, pastas, steaks and raw bar. We utilize wood fire cookery for much of the menu, highlighting what makes each ingredient special. Our 2 bars serve market driven cocktails, paying homage to the classics. Natural and classic wines flow, with something for all tastes. Gjelina is a great backdrop for any occasion- date night out, a single seat at the bar for a pizza or a formal celebration.  Brunch service is fun and thoughtful with dishes like our 9 grain pancakes, baked eggs and huevos rancheros. For event inquiries, please email us!

name: Estela
description: One of the most celebrated restaurants in the United States, Estela is an intimate downtown bistro from chef Ignacio Mattos that can be experienced in many ways, whether it’s a thrilling dinner in the dining room, a leisurely weekend brunch with friends, or a late-night drink at the bustling bar. Since opening in 2013, the restaurant has served several signature dishes that are now considered New York City classics, while always offering guests something new to try. Estela holds one Michelin star, among many other major accolades.

name: Scarr's Pizza
description: First opened in 2016 and located on Orchard Street, where the LES, Chinatown and Dimes Square converge, Scarr's Pizza has been called, "The best slice in New York." With a focus on all-natural and organic ingredients, owner and 2024 James Beard Nominee (Best Chef NY State) Scarr Pimentel has crafted not only the best tasting slice in the city, but the healthiest too. Skip the line, book a table and come in for a pie. There is a full bar and the dine-in menu features the crowd favorite Hotboi and Clark Kent as well as exclusive items like the Calzones.

name: Potluck Club
description: Potluck Club is a Cantonese-American restaurant founded by childhood friends who grew up in Chinatown with a mission of preserving the neighborhood's cultural heritage.Reservations are recommended, walk-ins are welcomedWe have a 10 minute grace period for all reservations.  If we are able to accommodate you in the event that you arrive beyond the grace period, we will happily do so, however this is at the discretion of the host. If you are running late, please reply to your confirmation text to let us knowTo ensure a smooth dining experience for all our guests, we kindly ask for your understanding regarding our table usage time limits.  Please review and agree to the following guidelines before your visit1-2 guests: 75 mins3-5 guests: 90 mins6+ guests: 2 hoursParties 8+, please e-mail us events@potluckclubny.com to book your Prix Fixe menu reservationCancellation fee of $10 per guest will be applied for any cancellations within 24 hours of the reservation time, & no show

name: Phoenix Palace
description: The team behind Potluck Club opens Phoenix Palace, a sister restaurant, in the heart of Chinatown. The menu continues to celebrate Cantonese cuisine while drawing inspiration from their childhood favorites.

name: l'abeille
description: Nestled on a charming Tribeca cobblestone street corner, l’abeille is an intimate sanctuary where French culinary artistry meets Japanese finesse. With only 48 seats, this hidden gem captivates diners with its seasonal Chef’s Tasting Menu, meticulously crafted by the visionary Chef Mitsunobu Nagae. Drawing on his rich experience from Michelin-starred establishments in Tokyo, Paris, and New York, including a recent tenure as Chef de Cuisine at Shun in Midtown Manhattan, Chef Mitsu curates a symphony of flavors that transcend borders.At l’abeille, guests experience a harmonious blend of French sophistication and Japanese precision. Complementing the exquisite tasting menu is a rotating selection of seasonal a-la-carte dishes, thoughtfully paired with selections from the wine cellar and a captivating cocktail program. Immerse yourself in an unparalleled dining experience at l’abeille, where each dish tells a story and every moment is a celebration of culinary excellence.

name: Blu on the Hudson
description: Blu On The Hudson, perched on the Weehawken waterfront, is repeatedly hailed by guests as one of the most spectacular dining experiences in the New York & New Jersey area. Its soaring floor-to-ceiling windows frame uninterrupted, million-dollar views of the Manhattan skyline that many reviewers swear beat anything in the city itself—sunsets and city lights reflecting on the Hudson create pure magic. The sleek, modern 16,000-square-foot space feels upscale yet warm, with a lively bar, live DJ on weekends, and service that’s attentive without being stuffy. Food lovers rave about the inventive contemporary American menu: perfectly cooked steaks, handmade pastas, chilled seafood towers, and truly outstanding sushi. with the freshest options. Cocktails arrive as a mini show—smoke, fire, glowing orbs. Guests call it “romantic perfection,” “a hidden gem,” and “worth every penny and the ferry ride across the river”—an elegant, delicious escape that leaves everyone enchanted.

name: Casa Mono
description: Casa Mono and its sister restaurant, Bar Jamon, are located next to each other on Irving Place and 17th Street, just east of Union Square. Awarded three stars from The New York Times in 2015 and a Michelin Star in 2009 (and every year since), Casa Mono is a tapas bar restaurant inspired by the cooking of Spain’s Costa Brava.***Keeping our guests and staff safe is our number one priority.We are strictly following CDC, OSHA and NYC Health Department guidelines.***

name: The Tavern at Gramercy Tavern
description: Revered as one of the country’s great American restaurants, Gramercy Tavern has welcomed guests for over three decades with contemporary seasonal cuisine, warm hospitality, and thoughtful service. Rooted in the rhythms of the harvest and shaped by enduring relationships with local farms and the nearby Union Square Greenmarket, Chef Michael Anthony’s ever-evolving menus unfold across two distinct experiences. The Tavern offers wood-fired, seasonal dishes served à la carte in a lively setting.

name: Sunn's
description: Sunn’s is a cozy place for banchan and wine in Chinatown. Chef-owner Sunny Lee offers a selection of banchan alongside a rotating menu meant to be shared. Soju, makgeolli and ice cold Korean lagers can be enjoyed alongside the thoughtful wine list overseen by Parcelle.

name: Altro Paradiso
description: From globally acclaimed chef Ignacio Mattos, Altro Paradiso is an Italian restaurant in the heart of SoHo and an essential New York City dining destination. The restaurant’s menu draws inspiration from several Italian regions, celebrating timeless craftsmanship throughout, while the room’s high ceilings and glimmering brass bar contribute to a lively nightly atmosphere. Since opening in 2016, Altro has garnered many devoted regulars, ranging from neighborhood residents to travelers who treat the restaurant like a second home whenever they’re in town.

name: Muku
description: Muku (“無垢”), meaning “purity” or “innocence,” is a Japanese restaurant in New York City led by award-winning Chef Manabu Asanuma, whose experience spans acclaimed kitchens in New York, Japan, and Taiwan. Rooted in seasonality, simplicity, and intention, Muku draws inspiration from kaiseki while celebrating "goho" - the five classical techniques of Japanese cooking: raw, grilled, simmered, steamed, and fried. Each dish is crafted with restraint, using only what enhances the true character of the ingredients, resulting in a focused, expressive cuisine that honors tradition while offering a refined, modern Japanese dining experience.Guests are welcome to bring bottles not featured on our beverage list to enjoy with our menu. Corkage is $175 per 720ml sake bottle or 750ml wine bottle, with a limit of two bottle per reservation. Please email us at info@restaurantmuku.nyc to confirm whether we carry a label.

name: Fairfax
description: Birds, Burgers and Booze. "We've Got it All"Fairfax is an all-day neighborhood restaurant. Come through and hang!

name: ASKA
description: Chef Fredrik Berselius brings his love of nature to his urban surroundings through food at Brooklyn's only two Michelin-starred restaurant. With an emphasis on ingredients from the Northeastern region and influenced by his Scandinavian homeland, Aska, Swedish for "ashes," offers a distinctive dining experience through a procession of courses prepared from an open kitchen and served to guests of only ten tables each night.

name: The Dining Room at Gramercy Tavern
description: In the Dining Room, a warm and welcoming setting frames a more refined expression of our seasonal menu. At dinner, the menu is centered around a five-course, prix fixe seasonal experience. At lunch, guests may choose between a three-course prix fixe or à la carte.Revered as one of the country’s great American restaurants, Gramercy Tavern has welcomed guests for over three decades with contemporary seasonal cuisine, warm hospitality, and thoughtful service. Rooted in the rhythms of the harvest and shaped by enduring relationships with local farms and the nearby Union Square Greenmarket, Chef Michael Anthony’s ever-evolving menus unfold across two distinct experiences.

name: Joseph Leonard
description:
In its bright and airy modern bistro, Joseph Leonard serves modern American fare with a rustic twist. Appetizers include a charcuterie board with meats and cheeses, caramelized cauliflower with mustard vinaigrette and grilled asparagus with shrimp. Popular entrée items, such as the roasted chicken with English peas, steamed mussels topped with bacon in a white wine sauce and hen of the woods mushrooms with potato gnocchi, speak to the variety and high-quality ingredients utilized at Joseph Leonard. In addition to typical items like strawberry shortcake and chocolate mousse cake, a gourmet selection of cheeses are on the dessert menu.

Craft beer and specialty cocktails add to the contemporary feel of Joseph Leonard. Try the gentleman caller, with bourbon, peach tea, lemon and honey for a cool and refreshing addition to your meal.

name: Jeffrey's Grocery
description: Of all Gabriel Stulman's spots, Jeffrey's Grocery is the one you go to snack on raw bar offerings and indulge in a buttery, lobster roll. As with all Stulman joints — the vibe is lively, the service is delightfully friendly, and the specials are very worth considering. It's a go-to spot for a casual, fun-filled evening, particularly if you're in the mood for oysters, which they source from dozens of local aqua-farmers.

name: Parcelle Chinatown
description: Parcelle Chinatown, located at 135 Division Street, is a great spot for dinner or a casual wine-and-snack hangout, and if you're feeling spontaneous, there's always room for walk-ins.The upstairs main room features vintage furniture and cozy seating. Known for their robust wine list that highlights both classics and unique finds.Downstairs, you'll find their Studio Room, an intimate venue used for private events. When not booked, it transforms into a cocktail bar open to the public Thursday through Saturday.

name: Tolo
description: Tolo is a restaurant by Ron Yan, a Chinese chef that has spent the past decade working in NYC’s culinary scene. Tolo’s menu features a range of dishes from raw seafood to rice and noodles to sweet & sour crispy fish amidst vintage furniture and the backdrop of the open kitchen. Since then, it has earned recognition from Esquire’s Best New Restaurants in America, The Infatuation’s Best New Restaurants in NYC, and the Michelin Guide.The wide-ranging wine list with over 300 selections is curated by Parcelle. It spotlights natural producers as well as a deep bench of traditional collectibles. The contact phone number is TEXT ONLY. All large parties of 7 - 16 people are subject to the prix-fixe menu.

name: Momofuku Noodle Bar - East Village
description: Momofuku Noodle Bar East Village serves a constantly changing roster of noodles, steamed breads, and daily dishes. We offer reservations and also accept walk-ins. Reservations open 14 days in advance at 10 am. Make sure to keep an eye on our website for today's menu. Noodle Bar offers three large format meals:Fried Chicken Meal: Two whole fried chickens (southern-style and Korean-style) served with scallion pancakes, vegetables and sauces. Available for parties of 4 to 6 people at lunch & dinner. Look for "Fried Chicken" when reserving. Fried Chicken & Caviar: Two chickens battered and fried whole, served with caviar, chive crepes, potato chips and sauces. Available for parties of 2 to 4 people at 5pm and 8pm. Look for "Caviar/Chicken" when reserving. Whole Suckling Pig: Whole suckling pig platter with ssäm fixings, sausage & bean stew, rice and steamed buns. Available for parties of 6 to 8 people at 5pm everyday. Look for "Suckling Pig" when reserving.

name: Demo
description: Demo is a restaurant in the West Village serving modern, Europe-inspired cuisine from Executive Chef Quang Nguyen and Sous Chef Dina Fan. Inventive seasonal dishes—like wax beans with bottarga and boquerones—share the menu with updated classics like beef tongue tonnato, alongside a carefully curated list of wines by glass and bottle. The sleek interior, tastefully adorned with red marble and dark wood, hosts a front bar for walk-ins as well as a chef's counter in the back.Please note: We are a small restaurant. Reservations in the dining room and at the chef's counter are intended for dinner. For drinks and snacks we accept walk-ins at the front bar. Thank you for your understanding.

name: Parcelle Greenwich Village
description: Parcelle Greenwich Village is a restaurant from Parcelle, an online wine retailer and hospitality group, located at 72 MacDougal St. There is seasonal outdoor dining, a spirits list, full dinner menu, and wine list of over 500 selections.

name: Mitsuru
description: Mitsuru is a neighborhood Japanese restaurant in the West Village. We offer a range of Japanese fare focused on sushi, hand rolls and heartier entrees alongside a big wine list of 500+ bottles. An omakase can be enjoyed at the 8-seat counter, where chef-owner Mitsuru Tamura presides nightly.

name: Restaurant Le B
description: Restaurant Le B is an award-winning fine dining destination in the heart of the West Village, New York City, helmed by acclaimed Chef/Proprietor Angie Mar. Restaurant Le B is one of only eleven restaurants in New York to be awarded Four Stars by Forbes, and has also received Best New Restaurant accolades from Robb Report, Condé Nast, and the Financial Times, with Esquire Magazine naming its bar program among the 50 best in America.Set in a stylish, supper-club setting inspired by over 100 years of Lower Manhattan dining history, Le B offers a Continental menu interpreted for the modern diner. Chef Mar's hyper-seasonal cuisine marries Eastern flavors with French technique, presented through dramatic tableside preparations and forgotten pleasures. The iconic Le Burger is a staple, which Vogue dubbed as "the Birkin bag of burgers." As part of the James Beard Award-winning G.O.A.T. Series for MasterClass in 2025.

name: Pig and Khao - UWS
description: The exciting follow-up to LES favorite Pig & Khao from Chef Leah Cohen (Top Chef Alum, judge on Beat Bobby Flay). Whether you're going to the Museum of Natural History, a show at the Beacon Theater, or looking for a new neighborhood spot, we've got you covered! Our Southeast Asian menu is full of flavor and fun. Come in for our famous Sizzling Sisig and Coconut Rice or try our new roti tacos! We're open 7 days a week and serve brunch on Saturday and Sunday. If it's nice outside, we're outside - check out our sidewalk patio.Summer Days - join us starting at 12:00 pm on Thursdays and Fridays for summer specialsHappy Hour Mon-Wed - 4pm to 6pm!Kids Specials during Happy Hour!

name: The Modern
description: The Modern is a two Michelin-starred contemporary American restaurant located within the MoMA, overlooking the Abby Aldrich Rockefeller Sculpture Garden. The Modern’s seasonal tasting menus, curated by Executive Chef Thomas Allan, are complemented by an award-winning bar and wine program.Part of Danny Meyer’s Union Square Hospitality Group and a member of Relais & Châteaux.

name: odo
description: odo is open for lunch and dinner service from Wednesday through Sunday.The Lounge is open from Tuesday through SaturdayPlease note that reservations at the lounge do not offer our Kaiseki Tasting Experience.odo offers a 20 minute grace period.   Please contact us via email if you are running late.For the consideration of our other guests, children younger than 10 are not allowed at the Chef's Counter.  Please note that all guests seated at the Chef's Counter must partake in the tasting course.While we do our best to accommodate most allergies and dietary restrictions, we cannot accommodate vegan, vegetarian, wheat and gluten-free guests.  We are also unable to accommodate guests who cannot consume seafood or seaweed.Please contact us directly at odo.reservation@gmail.com to ensure we can accommodate your needs.Cancellations can be refunded up to 48 hours from the reservation.  Any cancellations made within 48 hours, as well as no shows will not be refunded.

name: Bar Kabawa
description: While we do offer reservations each night, we save half the room for walk-ins too. Come on by! | Bar Kabawa is a wine and daiquiri bar from Chef Paul Carmichael and Momofuku celebrating the flavors of the Caribbean in the heart of the East Village. Drawing inspiration from Chef Paul's culinary journey, the menu features daiquiris, wines by the glass, and a compact list of patties and raw bar bites. Bar Kabawa is a place to relax, enjoy, and feel at home—whether you're dropping in for a casual drink or gathering with friends.

name: Moody Tongue Sushi
description: ** PLEASE NOTE: Reservations for Moody Tongue Sushi are now also available on SevenRooms. Visit moodytongue.com/reservations to view availability and make a reservation. **Please e-mail newyork@moodytongue.com with any questions.----------Moody Tongue Sushi is MICHELIN Starred Moody Tongue Brewery's West Village restaurant focused on sushi and beer. The restaurant presents a hyper-seasonal, elevated sushi menu from Executive Sushi Chef Hiromi Iwakiri alongside curated beer pairings from Moody Tongue Brewery's acclaimed Brewmaster Jared Rouben. The beer menu showcases a rotating selection of 16 seasonal beers, including offerings like the Shaved Black Truffle Rice Lager, specialty beers such as the Pressed Asian Pear Saison (which is served in a flute and highlights notes reminiscent of vintage Champagne), and perennial favorites like the Orange Blossom Belgian Blonde and Yuzu Lager. The beverage program also includes a curated list of wines and cocktails.

name: Momofuku Noodle Bar - Uptown
description: At this second location, David Chang rethinks his iconic noodle shop. The Columbus Circle Noodle Bar serves a constantly changing roster of noodles, steamed breads made in-house, soft serve, and daily dishes, in a sleek wood-accented space. Bar-wise, there’s a full selection of beer, wine, sake, and cocktails, with a focus on highballs. Stop by for a quick bite at the kitchen counter, or book the private dining room for a celebratory event.Noodle Bar Uptown accepts reservations 30 days in advance starting at 10 am. Although making a reservation is recommended, walk-ins are welcome and can be accommodated at the bar or at tables if space permits.For parties larger than 9 guests, please visit the Private Events page on our website.

name: The Golden Swan
description: The Golden Swan is a neighborhood French tavern that occupies a historic West Village townhouse. The concept was inspired by an iconic New York restaurant of the same name that served as a den for artists and thieves in the 1900s. Chef Brixton's menu is rooted in sourcing, ensuring that every single ingredient that is served comes from the best possible place and is of the highest quality. Guests can casually walk into the first floor bar room, known as The Wallace Room, for the acclaimed Brixton Burger or find their way upstairs to The Dining Room for a more elevated experience with dishes served tableside like Dover Sole with Sauce Meuniere and Côte de Boeuf with bordelaise jus as well as signature dishes like Branzino en Papillote and Crescent Duck. No matter where you enjoy your meal, ingredient-forward drinks that harken back to the golden age of cocktails flow endlessly, evoking a sense of revelry that pays tribute to the concept's history.

name: Bar Miller
description: Nestled in an intimate space in the East Village, Bar Miller is the showcase omakase restaurant from the sushi concept Rosella. Just a few blocks away, our 8-seat sushi bar is seated twice nightly for a tasting menu that features local, sustainably-sourced ingredients.Domestic beer, wine, and cider shape a drink program that stays true to our approach to the menu, while the sake list highlights Brooklyn-based breweries and less conventional styles from Japan.Bar Miller happily accommodates solo diners. Please email info@barmiller with your reservation request and we will follow up!

name: Di An Di
description: Named for an endearing Vietnamese phrase shared between friends and family, Di An Di translates to “let’s go eat” and specializes in modern Vietnamese fare. Owned and operated by first-generation Vietnamese-Americans, the cuisine is rooted in tradition with a focus on innovation, led by Executive Chef Caroline Hahm. The restaurant's signature dish, Pho Thin Hanoi, is a northern-style pho that captures the essence of Vietnamese flavors. Di An Di has been recognized by the Michelin guide and is located near Transmitter Park in Greenpoint. The atmosphere is airy and verdant, enhanced by beautiful skylights that create an inviting dining experience.

name: The Bar Room at The Modern
description: Our full availability can be seen at themodernnyc.comLocated adjacent to The Museum of Modern Art, The Bar Room at The Modern offers Chef Thomas Allan's vividly seasonal contemporary, à la carte cooking, along with an award-winning wine program and carefully curated cocktails, beer, and spirits. Guests have the option of sitting in The Bar Room, the adjacent lounge, or at the vibrant 20-seat marble bar. Walk-ins are accepted in all three areas, and The Bar Room at The Modern welcomes reservations up to 28 days in advance.

name: Brass
description: A New York City Brasserie. Taking inspiration from the style of French brasseries but without adhering to their playbook, BRASS restaurant showcases a playful contemporary spirit while remaining true to the cuisine’s unwavering appeal.

name: Drift Restaurant & Bar
description: Drift Restaurant in Weehawken, NJ serves as a blend of modern flair and coastal charm.  It is elegantly perched on the waterfront, offering breathtaking panoramic views of the Manhattan skyline.  Drift's interior is sleek yet cozy, adorned with a nautical-themed de for that evokes a serene oceanic atmosphere.  The menu is a delightful journey through contemporary American cuisine.

name: Farm & Table
description: Join us for an exceptional dining experience in Albuquerque’s gorgeous North Valley. We offer seasonal dishes created from scratch with fresh ingredients sourced from local farmers and from our own on-site farm. Delectable dishes inspired by New Mexico’s growing season will satisfy the omnivore as well as the vegetarian. Full entrees and small plates are complemented by craft beer and a diverse wine list. Enjoy intimate indoor dining in the winter and fall and take advantage of our beautiful outdoor patio in the spring and summer overlooking our lush ten-acre farm. After your meal, take a walk through the farm or visit our sister business La Parada for unique one-of-a-kind treasures from around the world. See you soon!

name: The Pinery
description: The Pinery is a guest-driven, chef-inspired dining experience located on beautiful Lake Ivanhoe, in the heart of Orlando. Inhabiting land that once cultivated Florida’s former top export crop -pineapples- The Pinery celebrates Florida’s forgotten history while offering guests a charming blend of rustic elegance, industrial comfort, and serene lake views. Join us in Ivanhoe Village, take in the sunset by the lake, celebrate your friends and enjoy the beauty and bounty Orlando has to offer.

name: Norman's - Orlando
description: Norman's Florida Kitchen is the Orlando dining experience featuring the cuisine of James Beard awarded Chef and author, Norman Van Aken. Norman’s focuses on providing warm hospitality with a sophisticated ambiance, while serving the highest quality steaks and freshest Florida seafood, paired with an award-winning wine list and artisanal craft cocktails. Several dining spaces are available including the main dining room, two private dining rooms, an intimate bar/lounge, and an elegant covered open-air terrace for elevated dining.Multiple award-wins include ‘Best Restaurant’ and ‘Best Date Night Restaurant’.Located just minutes from the Orange County Convention Center and other major Orlando attractions, it’s the perfect place to enjoy a memorable evening.HAPPY HOUR: Daily 5-7pm with $10 Cocktails, $8 Wines by the glass, and $5 BeersDRESS CODE: Dining Room: Smart Casual/ Smart Dress (jackets are not required) Terrace: Resort Casual/ No baseball caps, gym apparel, tank tops

name: SkyHigh
description: Crowning the 60th floor of Four Seasons Hotel at Comcast Center, SkyHigh elevates Philadelphia's rooftop dining as the city's highest lounge. This upscale destination pairs panoramic views with approachable Jean-Georges cuisine, expertly crafted cocktails, and live music in an elegant setting. Recognized among Philadelphia's premier rooftop destinations, SkyHigh delivers exceptional dining from breakfast through dinner, including celebrated Sunday brunch with three-course menus and decadent dessert buffets. Whether seeking rooftop venues in Center City, high-end dining, or sophisticated cocktail lounges, SkyHigh offers an unforgettable experience above the clouds with world-class service and stunning cityscape views.

name: Vernick Fish
description: Vernick Fish is a refined Center City dining destination at the Four Seasons Hotel Philadelphia at Comcast Center, reimagining the American oyster bar with ingredient-driven cuisine and global inspiration. The menu highlights pristine oysters and sustainably sourced shellfish, presented through a seasonally evolving selection of expertly composed dishes. Recognized among Philadelphia's premier coastal dining destinations, it offers a polished experience complemented by curated cocktails and a wine program designed to enhance seafood-forward flavors.

name: Pine Street Grill
description: Pine Street Grill is a neighborhood restaurant serving timeless American food made with care. Located on a corner of Fitler Square, Pine Street offers thoughtfully prepared classics such as matzah ball soup, spinach artichoke dip, Greek salad, and rotisserie chicken.Our approach is simple: the same sourcing and care found at My Loup and Her Place Supper Club, with a focus on the core fundamentals that make food and drink memorable. Our beverage program carries this forward with classic cocktails, beer on tap (including birch!), and a tightly curated wine program with no pretense, just great drinkability at a variety of price points. Opening hours are Wednesday — Monday, 4-11pm (the kitchen closes at 10:30pm). Half of our dining room is available to reserve and the other half saved for walk-ins. Reservations open 30 days in advance at 9am ET daily. Happy hour is from 4 - 6pm daily and we also offer a PSG late night burger + beer special (draft beer + PSG burger for $20) nightly.

name: Friday Saturday Sunday
description: New American with a familiar name. Chef Chad Williams and his wife Hanna have taken a familiar spot and reinvented it.  Craft cocktails at the main floor bar and delicious and inventive dishes in the second floor dining room. Please be aware that we do not have an elevator. For diners with limited mobility we offer the full dinner menu at the bar, and seating is first come first served. Reservations for the entire month open on the first of the month prior at 10am via OpenTable.Please email with any questions, as our phone line is information only.

name: My Loup
description: Located just off Rittenhouse Square, My Loup’s menu is French Canadian with a lighter touch. Guests can fashion a meal from a selection of smaller dishes and a drink at the bar or commit to a giant plateau fruits de mer and anything else the kitchen decides to send out as part of their “Let Us Cook For You” menu — potentially a half chicken with matzah balls or a halibut tail with turnips, smoked butter, and clams. Executive Chef Alex Kemp works with what suppliers have fresh, with change viewed as a catalyst that propels the menu forward and fosters a dynamic energy that encourages experimentation.

name: Her Place Supper Club
description: July reservations available Sunday, 6/21 @ 6pm ET. We are fully committed for June reservations. Please add your name to Notify Me for any openings.Her Place is kind of like a restaurant but kind of like a dinner party. The menu changes triweekly, sometimes daily, sometimes a la minute. Imagine showing up to your mom's friend's house for dinner and just eating whatever she makes -- it's a little like that. The food leans French and Italian with some Jewish touches swirled in. We offer two seatings of 26 guests Monday - Friday, at 6pm and 8:30pm. Our maximum reservation is for 6 guests. We have wine and cocktails!!

name: a.kitchen
description: Located at 18th and Walnut, a.kitchen is a French inspired American restaurant rooted in seasonality and a deep love of wine. Led by Executive Chef Eli Collins, the menu moves from thoughtful small plates to refined lunch and dinner classics, all designed to pair beautifully with what is in your glass. Morning service features a seasonal breakfast, with weekend brunch offering a fresh take on daytime favorites.​Nationally recognized for its natural and terroir focused wine program, a.kitchen highlights iconic regions and emerging producers, with a knowledgeable team ready to guide your next pour. The bar offers a focused cocktail list and wines by the glass, making it ideal for a drink before dinner or a casual seat at the bar.​ In warmer months, enjoy sidewalk café dining with a relaxed European feel.
