Honored by Wine Spectator, The New York Times, Vogue, and the James Beard Foundation.

name: Mawn
description: Mawn is open 4 days a week and is a 28 seat BYOB in the heart of South Philadelphia's iconic Italian Market.  Receiving national recognition, Chef Phila was the recipient of a James Beard Award for Emerging Chef in 2025, one of Food & Wine's Best New Chefs Class of 2025 and named on the 25 Best restaurants in America list for The New York Times. Reservations are released on the 1st of each month at 12PM NOON for the following month.**** Please note they will book VERY FAST and not all trying at the release will be able to get through. This is due to the HIGH demand and VERY limited availability.If there is nothing showing on OpenTable, it does mean all available reservations are gone. USE the NOTIFY option to receive alerts as people cancel. There is no internal Waitlist. ****Mawn offers WALK IN ONLY Lunch Thursday- Saturday 11am-2:30 (2:15 last seating) Dinner Dishes are offered as additions daily. Lunch is a great way to experience Mawn if you cannot get a reservation.

name: Honeysuckle
description: Honeysuckle is more than a restaurant. It’s a network of community spaces centered around the values of ancestry, nourishment, and reclamation that was founded by two- time James Beard Award Semifinalists Chef Omar Tate and Chef Cybille St.Aude-Tate. Positioned in the North Broad area of Philadelphia, our Michelin Guide recommended space, offers a fresh perspective on food systems - expanding the idea of Afrocentricity in food by redefining the limits of a restaurant and celebrating the legacies and recipes of those that came before them.Offered in our main dining room, our multi-course tasting menu experience places an emphasis on supporting local Black farmers & Indigenous ingredients while highlighting the food ways & cultures that we are designed by.  Our a la carte menu is offered in our bar & bar lounge area only.

name: Almanac
description: Almanac is a secluded haven where precision meets passion. Inspired by the finest traditions of Japanese bar craft and the mystique of the American speakeasy, we offer an intimate setting for slow, intentional drinking. Our handcrafted cocktails—guided by the Slow Drinks movement—explore the meeting point of Japanese flavors and American terroir, creating experiences that are both timeless and distinctly of this place.Reservations must be confirmed via OpenTable or SMS to be held. This helps us accommodate walk-ins and avoid unnecessary cancellation fees. Unconfirmed reservations may be released to our waitlist. Late cancellations within 24 hours or no-shows are subject to a $20 per person fee.House Rules: - No parties larger than 4 and no split parties.- No seating incomplete parties.- Your table time may be limited to 2 hours.- Flash photos, lights, and phone calls are strictly prohibited.- No rowdy behavior. - Be mindful of your fellow guests in the building.

name: Vetri Cucina - Philadelphia
description: *Reservations are released on the first of the month for the following month: ie. On October 1st, reservations are released for the month of November at 11 am ESTLocated in a historic townhome in the heart of Philadelphia, Vetri Cucina is a unique space that invites guests to become part of our family. The intimate dining rooms evoke the charms of Italy with custom crafted features including hand-blown Murano glass chandeliers, Richard Ginori porcelain and Venetian glassware.Showcasing the constantly evolving, dynamic creativity of Chef Vetri and his team, Vetri Cucina serves a classic tasting menu at $165pp using the highest quality local and specialty ingredients. Vetri Cucina also offers a seasonal Forchetta menu which is a premium tasting menu experience at $215pp. These are the restaurant’s sole dining options, designed to provide each guest with a memorable, unparalleled experience.

name: High Street
description: Located at the corner of 9th and Chestnut in the heart of Washington Square West, High Street is a cozy neighborhood restaurant and bar centered on local grains and seasonal cooking.​ Our exhibition bakery showcases pastries, pizzas, and handmade pastas, all visible from our light filled dining room, creating a warm and inviting atmosphere.​Join us for brunch, lunch, or dinner, or stop by the bar for cocktails and small plates. Executive Chef Christina McKeough’s seasonal menu highlights local producers with fresh, modern takes on classic dishes.​Tucked behind the main dining room, our Back Bar is a cocktail focused space hosting seasonal events and pop ups. The Back Bar and Back Room are available for private dining and group events. ​Recognized by the Michelin Guide and named one of The Inquirer’s 76 Most Vital Restaurants of 2025.​Parties of seven or more require a credit card on file and participation in our Leave It To Us menu.​

name: Tabachoy
description: Tabachoy is a Tagalog nickname given to a friend or family member; it means chubby or fatty. We know it sounds mean, but Filipinos give each other inappropriately appropriate nicknames all the time. Your baby brother’s got some neck rolls? Tabachoy. Auntie’s six months pregnant? Tabachoy. Or your dad’s got a six pack, but just finished his third piece of bibingka? Tabachoy!We cook the food that got us made fun of in elementary school, when we opened our country crock “Tupperware” - the food that reminds us of home. We don't strive to make a better adobo than your mothers, but we do want to share ours with you.

name: Ogawa Sushi & Kappo Omakase
description: Experience an unforgettable journey through the artistry of Edomae sushi at Ogawa Sushi & Kappo, an intimate omakase restaurant in Old City, Philadelphia. Led by Head Chef Carlos Wills, each 23-course omakase dinner features highlights such as buttery Ōtoro, sweet Bafun Uni from Hokkaido, delicate Kinmedai, and our signature Homeyaki Ebi Tamago. Every piece reflects precise Edo-style techniques and balance, with fish flown directly from Tokyo’s Toyosu Market. Guided by Master Chef Minoru Ogawa, who travels to Japan monthly to source the finest seafood, Ogawa delivers one of Philadelphia’s most refined Japanese dining experiences. Elevate your meal with expert sake pairings, or explore our collection of Japanese whisky, craft beer, and premium sake. Discover why Ogawa is acclaimed as one of the best omakase in Philadelphia, where craftsmanship, tradition, and hospitality unite.

name: Fat Ox
description: Inspired by the rich culinary history of the Bue Grasso (fat ox) Festival of Carrù Italy, Fat Ox serves modern interpretations of traditional Italian dishes from acclaimed local Chef /owner Matt Carter.The sleek 7,000 sq/ft restaurant features hand made pastas crafted in house daily, slow-roasted meats and seafood, an international wine list and a full craft cocktail bar to make for a truly special experience.The newly renovated front patio and our beautiful courtyard patio are the perfect place to enjoy the fall weather.

name: The Mission – Old Town Scottsdale
description: Old Town Scottsdale’s heart beats to a Latin drum at The Mission where renowned Chef Matt Carter (Zinc Bistro and Fat Ox) brings a modern twist to Latin cuisine blending influences from Spain, Mexico, Central America and South America. Sip a signature margarita with unique flavors such as cucumber jalepeno or aguacate (yes, we said avocado) as you watch your Tableside Guacamole made to your taste from more than a dozen fresh ingredients. Home made tortillas, and salsas are made fresh daily to accompany an assortment of meats and seafood  prepared on a Plancha or a grill using pecan and mesquite wood that instills a rich flavor.  And save room for dessert including our pumpkin bread pudding or expresso churros.

name: Wrigley Mansion - Geordie's
description: Opulent 1930s ambiance, sophisticated menus and stunning views.Step into the world of 1930s grandeur at Geordie’s Restaurant & Lounge. Led by Chef Peter Kloiber, enjoy enticing seasonal cuisine served in an elegant yet welcoming setting by our gracious staff. Recently refreshed and restored, Geordie’s grand dining rooms and unique Chef’s Table transport guests to a more refined era while delighting in the thoroughly modern fare. Indulge in an unforgettable dinner surrounded by breathtaking views. Along the way, you'll discover why Wine Spectator recognized us as one of America’s Top 100 Wine Restaurants, thanks to an outstanding wine program that includes more than 18,000 bottles.

name: Christopher's at Wrigley Mansion
description: Chef Christopher Gross, a James Beard Award winner, is among the top chefs in the U.S. His accolades include, The Antonin Carême  Medal ,the Robert Mondavi Culinary Award of Excellence, being named one of America's 10 Best New Chefs by Food & Wine Magazine, and an 18/20 rating by Gayot.Juxtaposed against the historic Wrigley Mansion, Christopher’s is a modern glass-and-steel gem featuring Chef Gross's creative weekend Tasting and weekday Classics menus. Perched atop a hill, the restaurant offers stunning city and mountain views from every table, an intimate dining room adorned with local art, and an open kitchen centered around a wood-fired grill. With a lively chef’s counter and open-air patio, Chef Christopher and his team not only cook but also serve, providing a fresh, ever-changing menu that delivers a unique and unforgettable hospitality experience.

name: Lom Wong
description: Esquire | Best New Restaurants 2023 James Beard Winner | Best Chef Southwest 2025Phoenix New Times | Best Thai Restaurant 2023/2024 Lom Wong celebrates the diverse people and cultures of Thailand by cooking the types of food that one would find in villages and small towns across the different regions of the country. Additionally, we tell the stories of the amazing people who honored us teaching us their recipes. Our goal is to show another side of the cuisines of Thailand by being true to ingredients, techniques, and flavors. We hand-pound our curry paste, hand-squeeze coconut milk, and are sincere to the flavors Thailand. Additionally, we offer a fantastic natural wine list and cocktails that showcase unique Thai ingredients. Chefs: Yotaka and Alex Martin

name: Chilte
description: Bon Appétit's  | 24 Best New Restaurants in America 2023.We come from street beginnings, with off the cuff new wave riffs, and somewhat experimental Mexican cuisine. Finding common threads through food, time, and place we bringing inspirations from our childhoods, the southwest, and shared cultures to the plate.Inspired by tradition and driven with creative intention we are authentically us and genuinely yours - AlwaysChef Lawrence Smith

name: Course
description: Food Network ‘Chopped’ Champion Chef Cory Oppold and the culinary team take you on a food journey of both your taste and visual senses, using seasonality of products and a progression of flavor.COURSE offers an 8-course Chef tasting menu Friday and Saturday and a 5-course ‘choice of’ menu on Tuesday, Wednesday and Thursday. A special menu series called, of • COURSE, is offered as "experiences" with limited reservation availability. Please contact us for large group dining and private events.Not just a meal, COURSE is a dining experience.MENU SCHEDULE- Our menus change seasonally; all menus can be viewed on our website- CourseRestaurantAZ.com SUMMER: 7/10/26- 9/23/26- Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday: 5-course - $135++- Friday & Saturday: 8-course - $175++

name: Latha Restaurant
description: LATHA, Swahili for flavor, celebrates Africa and the diaspora’s rich and diverse cuisines. The menu curated by Executive Chef Digby Stridiron honors the interconnected journey of the foodways and culture of Africa, Brazil, the Caribbean, and the Southern United States. Our contemporary pan-African kitchen respects historical recipes and crafts cuisine from thoughtfully sourced seasonal ingredients that reveal the connections between Africa and the diaspora. It is a journey of discovery along the trails of our ancestors, telling the authentic stories of the foodways and how they have shaped eating habits and cuisine worldwide.  We invite guests to join us on a flavorful journey of discovery and appreciation. LATHA, in downtown Phoenix, in Heritage Square,  is a place to have a shared immersive experience with friends or family while enjoying food, drinks, music, and culture in our dining room, bar, or outside patio.  As a collective, we EAT, DRINK, and VIBE!

name: ELEVEN
description: Located in Pittsburgh’s Strip District, Eleven pays homage to the city’s history with its industrial backbone and polished interior. The Eleven culinary team crafts the ever-changing menu from the most vibrant seasonal products painstakingly sourced from farms across the region. Eleven’s offerings can be experienced in the well-appointed dining rooms or more casually at the bar with the Tavern menu. Every dish at Eleven places the ingredients at center stage, echoing the kitchen’s position in the heart of the building.Reservations are available up to 30 days in advance; we look forward to serving you!

name: Dolly Olive & Bar Dolly
description: Located in the heart of Downtown Portland, OR on the bustling corner of SW Alder & SW 12th, Dolly Olive & Bar Dolly center around Southern Mediterranean ingredients and flavors, with an emphasis on an in house bakery program, fresh made pastas, and Italian aperitifs. Dolly Olive sports an inviting atmosphere featuring soft leather banquettes, warm wood accents, and custom patterned tiles. Bar Dolly shares a kitchen with Dolly Olive and serves as a companion 21+ bar to next door to Dolly Olive. Bar Dolly offers an extensive collection of Mediterranean wines, cocktails and regional dishes. Bar Dolly offers the same food menu as Dolly Olive.We're currently serving dinner daily, with brunch and a midday aperitivo hour on weekends. Whether you're stopping in for a pre-show dinner, celebrating a special event in your life, or just popping in for a quick bite, you can expect an excellent experience, start to finish.

name: Oma's Hideaway
description: Oma's Hideaway is a vibrant, maximalist restaurant inspired by the life and spirit of Chef Thomas Pisha Duffly's grandmother, Kiong Tien “Tina” Vandenberg. Her ability to adapt, to make the most out of limited resources, and to stay creative while projecting warmth and brightness is the guiding force behind Oma’s Hideaway.Born during the pandemic as a pop-up in the Gado Gado parking lot, Oma’s became a permanent beacon of iridescent light in Portland’s Richmond neighborhood in 2021. The menu draws from Tina’s life story, taking influence from the craveable, soul-satisfying hawker food of Singapore and Malaysia and “turning up the volume” with exaggerated yet balanced flavors in Chef Tom’s signature style. Oma’s serves dinner accompanied by a seasonal list of hand-crafted cocktails and zero-proof drinks.

name: Shalom Y'all
description: Shalom Y'all is an exploration and celebration of the Levant. We combines flavors and dishes from the Eastern Mediterranean, North Africa, and the Middle East with the produce and proteins of the Pacific Northwest to produce a phenomenal menu. Some standout items include the grilled lamb chops in Greek vinaigrette, the Baharat spiced chicken kebab served over mejadara, and our silky hummus served with fresh baked pita.Shalom Y'all is located in the heart of Eastside Portland, Oregon and boasts a charming, industrial atmosphere featuring exposed cement walls flanked by warm wooden accents. You'll experience innovative yet approachable cuisine, an inspired menu of cocktails and beverages, all in a lively atmosphere.We are currently reserving seating in our Dining Room, our Bar (21+), and our Chef's Counter.

name: Jeju
description: Please note: We kindly ask that large parties of 8 or more participate in one of our pre-fixed, family style menus.Korean-American cuisine with a focus on wood-fired cooking techniques and whole-animal butchery, Jeju Restaurant is the newest addition to the Han Oak family of restaurants by Chef Peter Cho and Sun Young Park.

name: L'Orange
description: Named one of the New York Times America's Best Restaurants 2024! Tucked away upstairs in a charming old storefront and voted one of Portland's 'Best New Restaurants' in 2023, L'Orange features an intimate dining space that beckons you to immerse yourself in a different era. Chef Joel Stocks and his talented team will guide your palate through adventurous dishes that blend European culinary traditions with the finest local ingredients. Experience the taste of "Old Portland" with every bite. Enhance your dining experience with a thoughtfully curated selection of wines, personally chosen by Owner/Winemaker Jeff Vejr and his team. While our wine list boasts a European focus, we also showcase a variety of local wines, including the exceptional offerings from Jeff's own Golden Cluster label. Join us at L'Orange for a taste of history, innovation, and the vibrant flavors of the Pacific Northwest!

name: Arden
description: Arden is an idyllic retreat tucked into Portland's Pearl District. Arden delivers seasonal Pacific Northwest cuisine and an award-winning wine program featuring over 250 bottles, many with bottle age. Guests have the option to dine a la carte or enjoy the chef's prix fixe menu ($75): a shared experience that features 4 courses of seasonal cuisine. Coursed wine pairings are also available ($50). Arden is named in homage to Shakespeare's famous forest - a place to escape the hustle and bustle of the city and appreciate nature's bounty and beauty. The restaurant invites you to take an exiting culinary adventure through the Pacific Northwest, and explore the incredible range of ingredients that the region has to offer.

name: Canard - Burnside
description: Wine bar meets casual eatery in the heart of Portland's Central East Side. From the team that brought you Le Pigeon.

name: Nodoguro
description: Nodoguro is a Japanese specialty restaurant in the heart of Portland. Your dinner follows the rhythms of kaiseki (Japan’s seasonal tasting tradition) reimagined through the ingredients and spirit of the Pacific Northwest. The meal unfolds slowly across 20 courses, from delicate vegetables and raw seafood to warm broths, steamed dishes, grilled bites, and a deeply flavorful rice and sushi courses.

name: Xiao Ye
description: Xiao Ye is a unique dining destination in Portland, Oregon, celebrated for its innovative approach to first-generation American cuisine. With a menu inspired by the rich flavors that define American dining today, Xiao Ye offers nostalgic comfort food with contemporary culinary techniques.At Xiao Ye, diners can enjoy a variety of dishes that reflect the diverse backgrounds and cultural influences from Chef Louis and his team. From hearty classics to creative twists, the menu is designed to appeal to a wide range of tastes. Their ever-changing menu allows for creativity and showcases seasonal produce of the PNW. Be sure to try their masa & mochiko Mini Madeleines!The candlelit, cozy interiors with mismatched chairs, makes it an ideal spot for gatherings, from a casual dinner with friends, a family dinner, or special celebration. Whether you're visiting for the first time or returning as a regular, you can expect a warm welcome and attentive service from the team lead by Jolyn Chen.

name: Han Oak
description: Han Oak has launched it's Spring Menu! Chef Peter Cho's take on jipbap, home-style cooking inspired by the bounty of Oregon. Expect a table full of banchan and sizzling entrees, rooted in Korean culture. Han Oak is a communal, prix-fixe Korean restaurant emphasizing traditional flavors, seasonal ingredients, and fine technique. From their addictive hot pot to their mouthwatering dumplings, every bite at Han Oak is a taste of pure happiness.

name: Hayward
description: Hayward offers “New Northwest” cuisine built on hyper-local, hyper-seasonal ingredients, transformed through fermentation, whole-animal butchery, and zero-waste practices. The ever-changing menu celebrates peak seasonality, creating dishes that feel both fresh and familiar.In Carlton, Oregon, the 70-seat dinner only restaurant opens Wednesday–Saturday at 4:30 pm, spread across six distinct spaces named like rooms in a home: sip on the Porch, circle the Bar’s brick fireplace, tuck into the Living Room’s cozy banquettes, gather in the main Dining Room, share the Kitchen’s communal banquette, or linger on the covered Garden patio.Chef/Owner Kari, who left Michelin kitchens in San Francisco to get closer to the source, has become a rising star in Oregon wine country. Hayward was a James Beard finalist for Best New Restaurant in 2024, and Kari followed as a semifinalist for Best Chef (NW & Pacific) in 2025.

name: La Terrazza
description: La Terrazza, the newest addition to Umbria Hospitality Group, presents a delightful fusion of southern Italian and Mediterranean cuisine, complemented by an enchanting city skyline view. With seasonal delicacies, premium ingredients, and market-fresh produce, our menu showcases traditional favorites with innovative flair. Step into our inviting dining space adorned with floor-to-ceiling glass walls, or unwind at one of our two bars – one indoors and the other nestled in a patio enclosure. We aim to whisk you away to a culinary experience like no other. Patio seating is first come first serve for reservations.

name: Tamasha Modern Indian
description: ⭐ MICHELIN Guide 2025 | Esquire's Best New RestaurantExperience modern Indian dining at its finest at Tamasha Modern Indian in Raleigh’s North Hills. Owned by visionary entrepreneurs Mike Kathrani and Tina Vora, Tamasha offers an unparalleled culinary journey in a lavish space designed by Tina Vora. Executive Chef Bhavin Chhatwani, now Michelin-recognized and a 2025 James Beard Award semifinalist in the "Emerging Chef" category, leads the kitchen with a menu blending tradition and innovation. Celebrated as one of the “50 Most Beautiful Restaurants in America” by OpenTable and People Magazine. Tamasha is North Carolina’s only Indian restaurant to earn this Michelin recognition. With only 50 restaurants in the USA and around 200 worldwide honored, Tamasha is proud to be part of this elite culinary list. Guests can savor this elevated experience in the stunning main dining room, private spaces, chic bar or our outdoor patio complemented by crafted cocktails and a curated wine list.

name: Poole's Diner
description: Poole’s is a modern diner with reimagined comfort food. It originally opened as Poole’s Pie Shop in 1945, and since then has been a staple of Raleigh's food scene where generations of locals have gathered to dine, celebrate, and rub elbows. Since 2007, Poole’s has been under the stewardship of chef Ashley Christensen and her team, who have honored the spirit of the diner’s comfort food roots while elevating the culinary experience with an emphasis on seasonal, locally produced ingredients and attention to detail. The dedication to warm hospitality, exceptional flavors, and creative cooking has earned her two James Beard Foundation awards. Poole’s has grown over the years, expanding from the original footprint at 426 S. McDowell into the building next door. Guests can enjoy dinner seven nights a week at the original Double Horseshoe counter or the Main Dining Room. For a quick drink or snack, the lounge is the perfect place.

name: Death & Taxes
description: This restaurant, from renowned chef Ashley Christensen, is dedicated to the technique of cooking with wood fire. You can find a delicious wood-fired steak, of course; but much of the menu showcases the element of fire in more subtle ways. Don't miss dishes like roasted oysters with preserved lemon and chili butter; rabbit cotechino with embered kale relish, mustard frills, smoked rabbit jus; and luxurious pastas like the buttery agnolotti with grilled maitake, chèvre, lemon thyme.The space itself is sophisticated and sleek, and full of character. The building was established in 1907 and has formerly housed a funeral home and a bank (the restaurant's name is a hat tip to these former tenants). Don't miss the beverage program, including the extensive wine list. The wine cellar is housed in a former bank vault in the basement.

name: Herons
description: Herons, the signature restaurant at The Umstead Hotel and Spa in Cary, North Carolina, offers a refined dining experience for breakfast, lunch, and dinner just outside Raleigh. During the day, guests enjoy relaxed, seasonal fare in a light-filled dining room or terrace overlooking the hotel’s lake and breathtaking nature views.In the evening, Herons transforms into a Michelin-recommended and Forbes Five-Star dining destination led by Executive Chef Steven Devereaux Greene. The dinner experience highlights an artfully composed tasting menu alongside à la carte selections, featuring ingredients grown at The Umstead’s organic farm and sourced from regional purveyors. Complemented by an award-winning wine program, culinary cocktails, and seasonal sushi, Herons offers a synchronistic dining experience where art, nature and hospitality collide.

name: Hemenway's
description: Hemenway's, an icon in the Rhode Island food scene and the go-to destination for fresh, top-of-catch seafood has been serving both locals and visitors to the capital city for decades.

name: Waterman Grille
description: Offering a setting that is unlike any other in Providence, Waterman Grille is situated along the Seekonk River in a former bridge gatehouse that dates back to 1871. Serving dinner nightly, and brunch on weekends. Offering weddings and celebrations in an intimate, waterfront location.

name: Los Andes Restaurant
description: Los Andes Restaurant serves up flavorful Peruvian and Bolivian dishes. It has a contemporary, casual setting with warm lighting. Los Andes Restaurant is open for dinner Wednesday-Sunday, as well as offers lunch at 3pm on Sunday. Guests can feast on plates of mussels topped with a chalaca salsa, whipped potatoes filled with avocado or chicken empanadas as an appetizer. Creative entrees like de-glazed pan-seared sirloin steak, grilled salmon or a classic ceviche with tilapia, squid, clams and more will satisfy the palate.Los Andes Restaurant was featured on Phantom Gourmet and S&W. It has a full bar that serves a variety of libations, including cocktails. This establishment has a beautiful outdoor patio with a waterfall and coy pond for a relaxing feel. It welcomes reservations and offers take-out and delivery services for guests wanting to enjoy the food at home. This restaurant also provides catering for various events.

name: Gracie's
description: Gracie’s is committed to providing an unforgettable dining experience. Great lengths are taken to ensure each dish highlights local seasonal ingredients. Our team works closely with local farmers, fishermen, brewers, winemakers, and distillers to bring you the freshest ingredients available. Gracie’s team is trained to accommodate food allergies and a wide range of dietary restrictions. We are conveniently located in downtown Providence’s theater district (across from Trinity Rep and a short walk from PPAC). We are also close to the Dunkin Donuts Center, the RI Convention Center, Brown University, RISD, Johnson and Wales University, and Providence College.Thank you for choosing Gracie’s and supporting our passion.

name: Stella's - Richmond
description: Stella’s honors rustic & modern Greek cuisine, bringing the freshest ingredients & Mediterranean fare table side.

name: Canon
description: Michelin Guide rated neighborhood restaurant with creative and shareable cuisine and a year-round patio."With a clear vision, a striking aesthetic and distinctive, mouthwatering flavors, Canon restaurant in East Sacramento is a stellar and welcome addition to the city’s dining scene." - 4-Stars, Sacramento Bee "Find the convivial Canon sequestered away from the downtown bustle. This cool space that contrasts deep tones against bright whites is enveloped by the aromatic smell of smoke, so it’s no surprise that the house is packed most nights. Chef Brad Cecchi is the culinary mastermind and his menu is pivoted around playful small plates." - Michelin Guide

name: Ella Dining Room and Bar
description: From the family behind The Kitchen, OBO' Italian Table & Bar, and Sellands Cafe & Bar, Ella Dining Room and Bar is a Downtown Sacramento landmark and premier dining destination. Ella offers New American, farm-to-fork cuisine for lunch and dinner, featuring seafood, steaks, pastas, small plates, fresh oysters, and traditional caviar service. Guests can also enjoy wine from an award-winning wine list and seasonal, hand-crafted cocktails at Ella's renowned bar.Ella specializes in private parties, with versatile spaces for gatherings of all sizes and a dedicated events team to help plan every detail.Located within walking distance of the Convention Center, State Capitol, Golden 1 Center, and major theaters and hotels, Ella also provides convenient valet service for shows and events.

name: Camden Spit and Larder
description: Camden Spit & Larder, a modern London-inspired eatery, in Downtown Sacramento on Capitol Mall. Chef Oliver Ridgeway showcases the pristine ingredients of the surrounding region with its bold design, bustling bar scene, and prime private dining facilities. Camden is comfortable yet elevated. We are located just a few blocks from the Capitol and Convention Center and just steps from the Golden 1 Center. This is the perfect place to dine pre-show or concert. Camden S&L was a restaurant 'Recognized by Michelin' in 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024 & 2025.

name: Prohibition
description: Step back in time at PROHIBITION, a 1920s themed bar, restaurant, and live entertainment venue in Murray.21+ ONLY. Valid ID required for entry.Known for elevated dining, craft cocktails, and live entertainment, PROHIBITION offers a unique experience unlike any other bar in Utah. Reservations are recommended, and walk-ins are welcome when seating is available.Please note: patio seating does not have a view of the shows.Reservation Policy:Tables are assigned upon arrival. We are unable to seat incomplete parties. All guests must arrive within 10 minutes of the reservation time or the reservation may be cancelled.

name: Monte
description: Monte by Chef Martin Babio is redefining fine dining in Utah through a refined, mountain-driven tasting menu experience. Located inside Beehive Distillery, Monte offers both long and short seasonal menus built around the finest ingredients from local farms, foragers, and artisans. Each dish reflects a precise, thoughtful approach guided by seasonality and a strong sense of place. Intimate and detail-driven, Monte delivers a deeply personal dining experience rooted in Utah’s landscape.Due to the nature of our menu, we are unable to accommodate dietary restrictions. The menu is subject to change at any time without prior notice.If paying with inKind: please book in advance via OpenTable. We are reservation-only and do not accept walk-ins. At the end of your meal, simply inform your server.For parties of six or more, please contact us directly.Kindly allow 2–3 hours for the full experience.

name: Matteo Ristorante Italiano
description: At Matteo, we invite you on a culinary journey to experience the heart and soul of Italy right here in Salt Lake City. Our Italian restaurant is more than just a place to eat; it's a celebration of family, tradition, and the joy of sharing exceptional food with loved ones. At Matteo, we pay homage to our Italian heritage by bringing the authentic flavors of Modena, Italy to your plate. Our menu is inspired by the recipes and techniques passed down through generations of Matteo's family and perfected by Chef Damiano Carlotto. Every dish is an ode to the rustic, comforting, and soul-warming cuisine of Italy. Buon Appetito!

name: Cosmica
description: Cosmica is a buzzy modern Italian diner that serves pure Roman & Southern Italian dishes in the Central 9th neighborhood of Salt Lake City, Utah. We are located just across the street from TF Brewing in the Maven West Building. Cosmica's team is focused on sourcing the best ingredients and products we can for our guests. Our menu includes house-leavened pizza dough, fresh pastas, small-production, low-intervention wines, and more! 2025 50 Best Restaurants in the Country - The New York Times2025 50 Best Wine Restaurants in the Country - Wine Enthusiast2025 Business Leader of the Year - Zachary Wade: Operating Owner, Executive Chef

name: Sunday's Best at the Post District
description: Our Capital City just got more bubbly! We're excited to bring our high-energy, good vibes, chef-crafted, tropical oasis to Downtown Salt Lake and be a part of the thriving & vibrant Post District community. Get ready to enjoy the most appetizing and flavorful brunch favorites while escaping to a sun shining, birds chirping, DJ spinnin, champagne poppin’ resort style atmosphere! Our downtown location stands 2 Floors high, filled with lush interiors, gold accents, rich textures, multiple awe-inspiring outdoor atriums overlooking our majestic cityscape, and a never done before Cocktail & Champagne Rooftop Lounge! Sunday School, the +21 and older only bar upstairs, will make you feel like you're stepping into sunshine in this bright & beautiful lounge. It's your go-to destination for the first cheers to any night out! Look no further for the best brunch in Salt Lake City. A New Era is here!!

name: Clementine
description: At Clementine, we’re serving seasonal, innovative eats in a modern & lively atmosphere. Our team, led by John (2023 James Beard nominee for Best Chef: Texas) and Elise Russ, draws inspiration from diverse global flavors. Our ever-evolving menu guarantees fresh and exciting options, while our dishes like the White Mushroom Salad, Ricotta Cavatelli and Clementine Crunch Bar remain cherished favorites.  Our beverage menu is small but mighty, carefully curated to include only the most delicious wines & beers from our absolute favorite producers expertly selected to elevate your dining experience. Want your own bottle? Our corkage fee is $35 per 750ml.Our Dining Room provides comfortable indoor seating, while the Kitchen Bar boasts an exciting vantage point overlooking our open kitchen, allowing you to witness the creation of each dish. For a change of scenery, our covered patio, is perfect for al fresco dining, complete with heaters and blankets to keep you cozy on those cool nights.

name: Ironside Fish & Oyster
description: Little Italy was built by the fishing industry. Ironside attempts to continue that narrative. It's our contemporary interpretation of the classic fish house and oyster bar. Located in a historic auction house on India St we did a full comprehensive restoration of the building, with three open-air culinary hubs featuring an expansive raw bar, chefs table, and bakery. We tried to be mindful of the past while looking towards the future. Groups of 6 or more will be charged an automatic 20% gratuity. A 4% surcharge will be added to all guest checks. For group reservations of 7 guests or more, please contact the restaurant at 619-269-3033 or via email to info@ironsidefishandoyster.com.Ironside is now taking reservations for patio and dining room seating. We are now offering Happy Hour MONDAY THROUGH FRIDAY FROM 3:00PM - 5:00PM.

name: C-Level
description: Located on Harbor Island, C Level Lounge offers stunning views of the San Diego Bay, downtown skyline and Coronado Bridge. The expansive patio showcases canopy shades, overhead heaters and fire features including an outdoor fireplace on the charming Lanai.The California Coastal Cuisine menu crafted by Chef/Partner Deborah Scott features fresh and innovative seafood dishes and classic dishes for meat lovers.

name: Animae
description: Animae is a modern Asian steakhouse in downtown San Diego, located in the landmark Pacific Gate Tower. Led by James Beard Finalist Chef Tara Monsod, the restaurant blends wood-fired cooking with bold Southeast Asian flavors, drawing inspiration from Filipino, Thai, and Japanese cuisines.The menu highlights premium steaks alongside signature dishes like Wagyu with housemade sauces and shareable plates layered with spice, acidity, and depth. Seasonal ingredients and thoughtful sourcing drive a menu designed for both indulgence and balance.Inside, Animae delivers a striking dining experience. Inspired by the architecture above it, the space is a futurist take on Art Deco glamour—lush, cinematic, and high-energy. The dining room is designed for celebration, whether you're planning a date night, group dinner, or special occasion in the heart of downtown San Diego, just steps from the waterfront and the Gaslamp Quarter.

name: Coasterra
description: Coasterra is San Diego's premier bay front dining destination! We are an innovative and modern restaurant, lounge and floating event center, featuring Mexican cuisine.

name: Herb & Wood
description: Herb & Wood is a wood-fired restaurant in San Diego’s Little Italy. Led by Chef Brian Malarkey the restaurant is known for its ingredient-driven menu, seasonal cooking, and warm, design-forward dining experience.The menu centers around live-fire cooking, with signature dishes like housemade pastas, roasted vegetables, and wood-grilled meats and seafood that highlight the quality of locally sourced ingredients. Partnerships with nearby farms and fisheries shape a menu that evolves with the seasons, balancing simplicity with bold, satisfying flavor.Inside, Herb & Wood blends rustic warmth with modern elegance—featuring an open hearth, natural textures, and a spacious dining room that feels both inviting and refined. The restaurant also offers private and semi-private dining spaces, making it a go-to for group dinners, celebrations, and special occasions in Little Italy.

name: Juniper & Ivy
description: Featuring Executive Chef, Alex Penkin. Nationally-acclaimed Juniper and Ivy-consistently ranked "Best of the Best" by San Diego Magazine, and recognized as a Michelin Guide Bib Gourmand establishment-is a foodie paradise on the edge of San Diego's Little Italy. The kitchen delivers a constantly rotating, chef-driven menu that uses hand-selected, seasonal ingredients crafted with precision, passion, and creativity. Founded by Owner Michael Rosen, Juniper's kitchen is backed by a supergroup of culinary minds that produce one of the most refined yet imaginative menus in town, paired with an award-winning wine list from the city's top sommeliers.

name: Marisi
description: Discovering Italy by way of La Jolla, Marisi focuses on Italian hearth cooking with an emphasis on handmade pastas, fine wine and classic cocktails. The menu celebrates the rich diversity of cuisine from across Italy, fashioned in a time-honored tradition of handmade all day, every day. A dream that lives between the Italian and La Jolla coasts, suspended between tides instead of time — a mood more than a plot. A visit that feels a lot like coming home, if home was a warm, charming villa off the Amalfi coast. Marisi is a taste of something you want to remember. We hope you love it. Live Music from 11AM-2PM every Sunday.

name: Kettner Exchange
description: Kettner Exchange boasts two levels of indoor/outdoor drinking and dining and Little Italy’s only rooftop patio with spanning harbor views.  We feature Progressive American cuisine from critically acclaimed Executive Chef Brian Redzikowski. In 2019, Kettner Exchange was named to Michelin Guide's prestigious Bib Gourmand list.  Kettner Exchange has proudly maintained this award every year thereafter. Originally introduced in 1997, the Bib Gourmand category highlights restaurants that serve high-quality meals which include two courses and a glass of wine or dessert for $40 or less.

name: Island Prime
description: Located on Harbor Island, Island Prime offers stunning views of the San Diego Bay, downtown skyline and Coronado Bridge. The main dining room showcases floor-to-ceiling windows and is propped up on wooden stilts over the water, making it the only waterfront steakhouse in the city of San Diego.The traditional steak and seafood menu crafted by Chef/Partner Deborah Scott features premium cuts and fresh catches a la carte as well as composed entrees.

name: The Marine Room
description: Perched along the dramatic shoreline of La Jolla, The Marine Room delivers one of San Diego’s most iconic oceanfront dining experiences. Picture windows frame the ever-changing Pacific, where waves crash just beyond the glass, creating a mesmerizing, immersive backdrop. A destination for generations, this legacy restaurant blends timeless elegance with a fresh, coastal perspective, earning acclaim for its refined culinary program and intuitive service. Seasonal menus showcase the finest local ingredients, thoughtfully composed and beautifully presented. Whether for a leisurely sunset dinner or a spontaneous evening by the sea, The Marine Room embodies the spirit of coastal California dining at its most captivating—where flavor, atmosphere, and the rhythm of the ocean come together effortlessly.

name: Paradisaea
description: A celebration of food, cocktails, art, and warm hospitality. Housed in the historic Piano Building, in the heart of the vibrant community of Bird Rock, Paradisaea is a destination restaurant and bar, that San Diegans frequent for crave-able, accessible fare, executed at the highest level. MICHELIN Guide 2023, 2024, and 2025

name: Solare Ristorante Lounge
description: Solare Ristorante is a Michelin Guide 2026 restaurant - Wine Spectator Grand Award 2026 Winner - named "Top 5 San Diego Restaurant" 2026 by San Diego Magazine. Family-owned and operated, the Smerik Family and their team are dedicated to authentic Italian cuisine for over 13 years. Our Executive Chef is Denice Grande. Pasta from scratch is made every day.  Solare is open 7 days a week. Solare has a full bar and a deep wine selection. Both indoor and heated covered patio dining. Private room available. Parking is free and easy.

name: Casa Gabriela
description: Chef Gabriela Lopez brings her elevated Mexican cuisine to Downtown La Mesa.  Using her grandmother's recipes from Guadalajara crossed with her cosmopolitan upbringing in Tijuana, Chef Gabriela's menu features authentic hand-crafted dishes and imaginative takes on classic dishes.  All of Baja California is represented along with influences from other regions in Mexico.The spacious outdoor garden, cozy loft, and private dining areas are the perfect places to enjoy tequila and mezcal based cocktails, family celebrations, or brunch with friends.

name: The Whaling Bar
description: Rediscover The Whaling Bar in La Jolla, blending historic charm and modern luxury. Once beloved in the La Valencia hotel, it's back after ten years, mixing its rich legacy with today's elegance. Known for its cozy atmosphere and top-notch service, it's been updated for contemporary tastes without losing its heritage essence. Executive Chef Brian Redzikowski, with a background in America's top kitchens and Iron Chef America, leads the culinary journey, offering a menu that combines classic and innovative approaches. The Whaling Bar invites both old fans and new to enjoy luxury and become part of La Jolla's ongoing story.

name: KINDRED
description: Equal parts evil lair and Victorian cocktail salon, KINDRED has transfixed and beguiled all who step into its portal, with whimsical eccentricity, world class vegan dishes, and ambitiously inventive cocktails.Since opening its doors in late 2015 in the enchanting neighborhood of South Park, San Diego, KINDRED has garnered recognition which transcends its plant-based credo, heralded as one of the city's premier dining institutions. It has been named EATER San Diego's and San Diego Magazine's Restaurant Of The Year, topped Zagat San Diego's Most Important Restaurant Openings, and earned an Orchid Award for interior design from the San Diego Architectural Foundation. KINDRED was also named within the Food Network's list of the best vegan restaurants in the country, was voted the Best Vegan Bar in the U.S. by VegNews magazine, and received regional honors from the Tales Of The Cocktail Foundation for Best Restaurant Bar in the West.

name: Herb & Sea
description: Herb & Sea is a coastal California restaurant in Encinitas, just steps from Moonlight State Beach, known for its wood-fired cooking and seasonally driven seafood. Led by Chef Aidan Owens, the menu highlights locally sourced fish, shellfish, and produce from nearby farms, with a focus on sustainability and connection to the region.Signature dishes center around the restaurant’s “Fresh Catch” program, showcasing underutilized local species and supporting Southern California fisheries. From oysters and crudo to wood-roasted fish and housemade pastas, each dish reflects a balance of simplicity, flavor, and thoughtful technique.The space blends laid-back coastal energy with a warm, design-forward dining room and an open-air patio ideal for sunset dinners and group gatherings. Whether you’re joining for a casual meal, date night, or special occasion, Herb & Sea offers a distinctly Encinitas dining experience rooted in community, seasonality, and the ocean just beyond the door.

name: Lilo
description: A lively and immersive dining experience that welcomes you to celebrate life’s special moments, Lilo harnesses the untamed spirit of the California coast and the world's shorelines through a curated, seafood-focused multi-course tasting menu rooted in a thoughtful connection to nature,  served around a 24-seat chef’s counter.

name: Vintana Wine & Dine
description: Located on the Penthouse Level of the The Centre at Lexus Escondido, Vintana offers a modern take on California Cuisine and picturesque views of the town and countryside.

name: Jeune et Jolie
description: A culinary experience celebrating the moment, Jeune et Jolie reimagines French cuisine through the vibrant lens of our Southern California landscape with a 4-course menu offered at $120 per guest. For those looking for a more casual evening, we feature "Le Menu du Bar”, an à la carte offering available for walk-in guests at our bar and lounge.

name: Lark
description: Welcome to Lark, Seattle’s beloved fine-dining destination for 20 years, with New American cuisine from Chef John Sundstrom celebrating the bounty of the Pacific Northwest in a luxurious, comfortable atmosphere with impeccable, welcoming service. Visit our elegant space in the Capitol Hill neighborhood, easily accessible from Downtown Seattle, and experience our James Beard Award-winning food and hospitality.

name: Westward
description: Waterfront dining on the north shore of Lake Union, featuring outdoor fire pits, cheery beachside seating, sustainable seafood, and imaginative marine fare of the Pacific Coast-- including a variety of fresh, local oysters.Arrive by foot, vehicle, boat, kayak or tie up to our dock.We have a limited number of tables available for reservations inside in our dining room for parties of 8 or less.  Weather permitting, we do our best to move reservations to outside when desired.  For larger parties, we also offer a semi-private dining room that can accommodate up to 12 guests.   We look forward to hosting you at Westward!  We hope to see you soon.   Until we meet at the table....

name: Atoma Seattle
description: We are a contemporary restaurant that celebrates the diverse and global influences of our team's culinary background. Our mission is to provide a space that fosters and inspires creativity. From this environment comes a unique experience that pays homage to our surroundings. We take pride in the farmers, ranchers, fishermen, and winemakers that surround us in the Pacific Northwest. Drawing on our experiences, childhood, and travels, we aim to invoke a sense of nostalgia and thoughtfulness throughout your dining experience.Our cuisine is a true reflection of the seasons, drawing inspiration from the bountiful offerings of our surroundings. With a deep reverence for our culinary heritage, we present dishes that marry traditional European techniques and flavors with a contemporary twist. Every plate that emerges from our kitchen is a testament to our commitment to humble yet inspired creations.

name: TOMO
description: We serve eclectic Japanese-American cuisine and serious wine in a former adult video store. Since opening, Tomo has received recognition as one of the country's best new restaurants by Esquire and Robb Report and has been nominated for Outstanding Wine Program by the James Beard Foundation. Chef Brady Ishiwata Williams’ menus are personal, and feature outstanding ingredients prepared simply and with confidence. Our wine program highlights rare and ephemeral wines made by creative, principled people. We also serve a variety of classic and seasonal cocktails, emphasizing small-batch spirits and quality nonalcoholic options.We strive to be a cherished part of our guests' lives, providing a warm atmosphere, ever-changing menus, and exclusive events with top chefs and industry experts from around the world.

name: Takai by Kashiba
description: Led by James Beard Award nominee Chef Jun, Takai by Kashiba is a fine-dining Edomae style sushi restaurant specializing exclusively in nigiri sushi.Sushi counter dining is offered solely as Chef’s Omakase, with seatings at 5:30 PM and 7:30 PM. Omakase is also available in the dining room, along with select à la carte sushi options.Vegan, vegetarian, and children’s menus are not available. Please disclose any allergies at the time of reservation. While all reasonable precautions are taken, we cannot guarantee the absence of allergens. Due to the intimate nature of our space, we are unable to accommodate infants, toddlers, high chairs or strollers.

name: Olive + Oak
description: VIEW TODAY'S MENU HERE https://indd.adobe.com/view/4f193613-6d68-4eef-9d3c-177c76a63667. We set out to create a fun, lively, and approachable restaurant and bar in our own back yard — a friendly, bustling neighborhood spot where the very best in food and drink would be served in a welcoming setting. We are looking at every single detail to make sure that Olive+Oak will be the perfect place to enjoy great wine, craft beer, well made drinks and thoughtful, ingredient driven food with friends and family.

name: Madrina
description: An intimate venue in the heart of Old Webster Groves, the Madrina team is ready take our guests back to a time where dining out was a cherished experience shared by family and friends. We are proud to showcase our Italian heritage and love for human connection through a menu carefully curated by Chef Tim Adams and owners Frank Romano and Stanley Browne. We are so excited to have you!

name: Psomi
description: PSOMI literally translates to “bread” but its essence is so much more. Psomi refers to the best bite, the “choice” piece, the crumb that everyone at the table fights over. Whether you’re a local or just passing through- Psomi is more than just an incredibly delicious, beautiful bakery + eatery, it’s a place to call home.

name: Predalina
description: Predalina isn’t just a restaurant—it’s a state of mind. Rooted in alegria de viver, the joy of living,  it’s a place to escape, connect, and celebrate. Sip a spritz under the golden disco ball, savor coastal European flavors with a nod to Portugal, and feel transported to the Copacabana—right in the heart of Water Street. With bold food, flawless design, and an electric, effortless energy, Predalina redefines lifestyle dining in Tampa.

name: Fortu
description: Fortu is a culinary sanctuary showcasing modern Asian cuisine. Rooted in Japanese influences, our menu features bold, Asian-inspired dishes crafted with meticulously sourced global ingredients. Each creation balances tradition and innovation, designed to surprise and delight with every bite. Our sophisticated yet welcoming space, bathed in soft lighting, creates a sensory experience, enhanced by expertly crafted cocktails that elevate every occasion. Embracing our philosophy, “Savor the Journey,” Fortu delivers a dynamic fusion of flavor, culture, and connection, transforming every visit into a celebration of culinary excellence. Come, experience the essence of Fortu.

name: Il Ritorno
description: Italian for the discerning palate. Il Ritorno brings a new taste of fresh Italian cuisine to the community—introducing new, interesting flavors and combinations that add elegance to a traditionally rustic genre. We want our food to be enjoyed and memorable to all those dining at Il Ritorno. Therefore, we are well prepared to handle special requests and dietary restrictions. Speak with your server for options or questions.

name: Ava June
description: Ava June is a modern French-inspired brasserie located in the heart of Tulsa, Oklahoma. Led by Chef Trevor Tack, the restaurant blends Parisian elegance with a distinctly local sensibility, offering refined yet approachable cuisine rooted in technique and seasonality. The dining room features a lively raw bar, an extensive 300-plus bottle wine list curated by in-house sommeliers, and a sophisticated craft cocktail program that celebrates both classic and contemporary styles. Designed to evoke the warmth and charm of a European café, Ava June is a destination for those who appreciate exceptional food, thoughtful hospitality, and the art of dining well — morning to night, from espresso and oysters to Champagne and steak frites.

name: Amelia's Wood-Fired Cuisine
description: Amelia's Wood Fired Cuisine was opened in 2017 by owner Amelia Eesley, a long time restaurant professional, who wanted to bring to Tulsa the dining experiences she loved while living in New York.  Since then Amelia's Wood Fired Cuisine's has succeeded in producing top notch seasonal cuisine lead by Chef Andrew Donovan.  The experience at Amelia's blends the open wood fired kitchen with the beautiful candle lit ambience of the dining room; setting the stage for romantic nights, special occasions and celebrations.  We hope you agree and look forward to serving you soon.  Private dining is available.  Please inquire through the contact page of amelias.us.

name: LoFi
description: Lofi is Tulsa’s go-to spot for great vibes, good food, and even better company. Known for its creative pizzas and shareable small plates, it’s the perfect place for a date night, a night out with friends, or a chill evening fueled by great wine and music. With over 300 bottles on the wine list and a menu of craft cocktails, there’s always something new to discover and pair with your favorite bites. Live DJs spin vinyl on vintage audio gear, filling the space with warm, soulful sound. At Lofi, it’s all about slowing down, connecting with your people, and enjoying the simple things—great food, great drinks, and great music in the heart of Tulsa.

name: Stage Kitchen & Bar - Boca
description: Flavor forward cuisine crafted through intuition... STAGE (/STAAHJ/) We welcome you to Stage—an integrated, small-plates experience from James Beard Foundation Award-nominee Chef Pushkar Marathe, inspired by diverse culinary influences from across the world. Discover innovative dishes interwoven with tradition, utilizing fresh and often local ingredients that come together in vital, new and flavorful ways.

name: Nicholson Muir Meats
description: At Nicholson Muir, Chef James Muir presents refined coastal cuisine rooted in technique, restraint, and time. Recommended in the MICHELIN Guide Florida 2026 for a second consecutive year and recognized as an OpenTable Diners' Choice restaurant, Nicholson Muir combines Argentine live-fire cooking with Italian culinary tradition.The menu features prime dry-aged steaks, Wagyu, house-made pastas, seasonal seafood, and chef-guided omakase experiences, complemented by a curated wine program and composed cocktails. Grilled selections are cooked over Argentine quebracho and natural charcoal, reflecting Chef Muir's long connection to asado and live-fire cooking.Set within the historic Ruth Jones Cottage in downtown Boynton Beach, Nicholson Muir offers warm, attentive hospitality and an intimate dining experience designed for conversation, balance, and unhurried evenings at the table.

name: The Butcher's Club at PGA National Resort
description: Stylish and modern elegance awaits you at The Butcher’s Club, with succulent cuts served in a timeless atmosphere of warm gold and 1950s swanky sophistication. Expect unforgettable culinary experiences at this modern Palm Beach shared-style steakhouse restaurant from celebrity chef, Florida native Jeremy Ford, James Beard Award nominee, and winner of Top Chef Season 13.Flavor South Florida tasting menu will be available September 1st - 30th Sunday through Thursday.

name: Taki Omakase - Delray
description: Taki Omakase is Delray Beach’s premier Japanese Omakase restaurant. We offer a curated 18-course chef’s choice menu - including traditional Japanese nigiri and cutting-edge, innovative seafood & wagyu dishes. We have 3 nightly seatings Friday & Saturday at 5:00, 7:00, & 9:00 pm. Tuesday-Thursday & Sunday we offer 2 seatings at 6:00 & 8:00 pm. Lunch seating available on Friday, Saturday and Sunday's at 12:30 pm.  I understand Dinner Omakase is $180 per person  + tax & gratuity. Lunch Omakase is $100  per person  + tax & gratuity. A deposit of half the Omakase ($100 at dinner $50 at lunch)  is required to book and will be applied toward the bill. ***Any changes or cancellations occur WITHIN the 24 hour period of your reservation day and  time will forfeit  your deposit/refund. ***All guests must consume raw fish.  We cannot accommodate vegan or vegetarian restrictions.

name: Dauphine’s
description: Dauphine’s is a modern day incarnation of the dramatic Grande Dame establishments of New Orleans, brought to you by the award winning Long Shot Hospitality - the team behind DC's beloved The Salt Line Restaurants. Dauphine’s utilizes Mid-Atlantic ingredients to prepare classic New Orleans fare while also creating original dishes inspired by the legendary city. All brought to you in a breathtaking spacious downtown restaurant setting with the most beautiful bar in downtown DC, plenty of patio dining, and a festive outdoor bar and garden area. Located in the Midtown Center, Dauphine's is just steps from the White House, National Theater, Convention Center, Cap One Arena and most downtown attractions and provides an elegant yet relaxed dining atmosphere for any occasion. Dauphine’s serves Dinner seven nights a week and Brunch on weekends, with Happy Hour every day 4pm-7pm and an ALL DAY Happy Hour on Sundays - plus don't miss our signature Sunday Live Jazz Brunch! We hope to see you soon!

name: Amazonia
description: Venture into the relaxed ambiance of Amazonia, our inviting upstairs bar, dining room and rooftop hideaway. As one of the first Amazonian restaurants in the U.S., discover the exotic flavors and ingredients from Peru's rainforest, as well as favorite Peruvian staples. Here you can explore a thoughtfully curated selection of pisco and imaginative cocktails. The dishes you'll enjoy in Amazonia are meant for sharing, and are expertly prepared by the very same Michelin awarded kitchen team from Causa downstairs.We offer our Sour Hour happy hour at are bar and bar tables Monday thru Friday from open till 7 pm. Bar seating is available first come, first served. Reservations are not available for Sour Hour.

name: Bresca
description: Bresca offers A La Carte and Tasting Menu options! Parties of 5 or more will be required to do our Chef's Tasting Menu. We are a Contemporary French restaurant and cocktail bar located in the bustling 14th Street neighborhood.  Our team explores the finest in seasonal ingredients paired with genuine hospitality.

name: Thip Khao
description: Thip Khao is acclaimed chef Seng Luangrath's flagship restaurant in Columbia Heights, Washington, DC. Thip Khao serves up spicy Lao (or Laotian) dishes and classic cocktails with an exotic twist.  Lao cuisine leverages tropical herbs and cooking techniques of Southeast Asia.  However, the flavor profile is distinct from its fellow SE Asian countries like Thailand, Vietnam, and Malaysia. Dishes like Naem Khao (Crispy Rice Salad), Sai Oua (lemongrass pork sausage), Laab, and Tam Muk Houng (Lao papaya salad) at Thip Khao showcase the unique flavors of Laos.  Many dishes are served with sticky rice. Chef Seng recommends eating sticky rice with your hands as most Laotians do.  Since its opening, the restaurant has been well received by Washingtonians and critics alike. The restaurant has had national recognition from multiple publications including New York Times, Washington Post, Bon Apetit magazine, Washingtonian magazine, and Michelin Guide (Bib Gourmand).

name: Elmina
description: Bold flavors. Rich stories. Unforgettable culture. Elmina in Washington, DC is Top Chef finalist and Food Network star Eric Adjepong’s modern Ghanaian restaurant, inspired by the historic coastal town in Ghana. Here, West African tradition meets culinary innovation in dishes crafted with depth, meaning, and extraordinary flavor. Signature dishes include our Jollof Duck Pot and Egusi Stew Dumplings, reimagining West African classics with fine dining finesse. The atmosphere blends warm Ghanaian hospitality with modern elegance, accented by rich textures, natural woods, and vibrant art. Guests can enjoy intimate dining indoors or relax in our cozy outdoor patio, perfect for a romantic evening or lively gathering. Please be advised: For Friday and Saturday evening reservations for parties larger than 4, we offer limited availability. If you cannot find a suitable time, please call the restaurant directly.

name: Xiquet by Danny Lledó
description: Xiquet (chee-KETT) by Chef Danny Lledó features a sought-after tasting menu showcasing the finest products from around the world served with warm, Mediterranean hospitality. Known as one of the best fine dining restaurants in Washington DC for its modern Spanish cuisine, Xiquet has held a MICHELIN Star since 2021 and earned MICHELIN’s Sommelier of the Year Award in 2022. In the same year, AAA honored Xiquet with the travel authority’s highest rating, making it the only Five Diamond restaurant currently in the city.  Recently, in 2023, the DC Restaurant Association awarded Xiquet with the RAMMY of Best Formal Fine Dining Restaurant of the Year.Please note that we do accept reservations for parties including 5 or more guests, however please contact us through email at info@xiquetdl.com and one of our sales managers will be in contact with you promptly to set your reservation up.

name: Causa
description: Causa is our Michelin-Starred tasting room where the world of Peruvian culinary tradition comes to life, thanks to the artistry of Chef Carlos Delgado. Our current menu, a Journey Through Peru, highlights dishes and ingredients from various regions and sub regions throughout Peru. We are also now offering a vegetarian experience and happy to accommodate almost all dietary restrictions (gluten and dairy allergies cannot be accommodated) We offer an amazing 6 course wine pairing or non-alcoholic pairing as well.Reservations can be booked two months in advance. This prix-fixe menu is $179 (tax & gratuity not included). If you have any questions or clarifications on allergens or restrictions, please reach out to info@causadc.com.Gift Card? Please use a credit card for your reservation and we will refund you at the end of your experience. .

name: Service Bar
description: Service Bar, opened by Chad Spangler and Glendon Hartley, has provided Washington DC with a relaxed and intimate alternative to the typical stiff-upper-lip cocktail bar.

name: El Taller del Xiquet
description: El Taller del Xiquet, or “Xiquet’s workshop,” celebrates Spanish flavors with a dedication to quality. Chef Danny crafts imaginative dishes from the finest ingredients, showcasing the vibrant cuisine of his roots. Our menu offers a seasonal selection of à la carte items and shareable plates, inviting you to explore the richness of both land and sea.Inspired by the traditions of coastal Spain, each dish reflects a respect for local agriculture and sustainable practices. This commitment is seen in premium proteins and seasonal produce from the Valencian region, highlighting the essence of Spanish gastronomy. Delicate octopus, sweet scallops, and succulent prawns are prepared with artistry and passion, while dishes like sustainable sea bass gravlax, signature paellas, and generous fideuàs are served tableside. El Taller del Xiquet offers a culinary journey into Spain’s traditions, honoring the ingredients and techniques that have graced tables for generations.

name: The Duck and the Peach
description: The Duck & The Peach is a New American restaurant in D.C.’s Capitol Hill, inspired by the fresh flavors of California and the warmth of New England. Led by Executive Chef Katarina Petonito and Rammy's Pastry Chef of the Year Rochelle Cooper, our seasonal menu features signature dishes like rotisserie duck with watercress panzanella and elegant, handcrafted desserts.Enjoy the full menu at our bar, offering craft cocktails and a curated wine list featuring women-made selections. Dinner is a celebration of the seasons, while our casual weekend brunch invites you to gather with friends and family. The space feels like stepping into a dear friend’s home—charming, warm, and welcoming.Join us at The Duck & The Peach, where every meal is a meaningful celebration of food, connection, and community. Named a James Beard Finalist in 2024 and one of D.C.’s 25 best restaurants by The New York Times, we can’t wait to welcome you.

name: The Ruxton
description: Curated by Aaron Taylor, Atlas Restaurant Group’s Corporate Chef & Chef Partner in this concept, the menu puts steak in the spotlight with over 11 cuts from Chicago-based meat purveyor Meats by Linz. The Ruxton will serve a mix of wet and dry aged prime cuts of Black Angus Beef, as well as dry aging Long Island Duck and Bison from Virginia’s New Frontier Farm in-house. To complement the steak options, seasonal shellfish and seafood entrees will be available including dover sole, Maryland rockfish, whole live lobster, king salmon, Chilean sea bass and more.Looking to elevate your dining experience, book your reservation in our beautiful wine cellar here: https://theruxtonsteakhouse.com/the-wine-cellar-experience/The Ruxton will serve lunch every weekday and dinner 7 days a week. Be sure to visit  Atlas’ new world-class cocktail bar and lounge next door to The Ruxton called Order of the Ace.

name: Azumi
description: Opened in 2014, Azumi is located on Baltimore's Inner Harbor in the elegant Four Seasons Hotel. With picturesque panoramic waterfront views, Azumi offers authentic Japanese cuisine with a strong focus on sushi matched with an amazing "hot kitchen." Azumi's Sake and Japanese Whiskey program is one of the largest in the region. Named the city's best Japanese restaurant by Baltimore Magazine, Azumi is also one of the hottest spots for nightlife in the city featuring a live DJ on weekends. Enjoy outdoor dining on our expansive patio. Outside seating can be requested but not guaranteed.Azumi’s highly-skilled teppanyaki / hibachi chefs are on display in our FLAME ROOM as they cook the highest-quality meats, seafood, vegetables & rice on a solid-surface iron griddle right before your eyes in this intimate & interactive dining experience. Please call the restaurant to book parties of 8 and larger or for additional reservation assistance.

name: MARTA
description: Tucked away in the charming heart of Butchers Hill community in Baltimore, MD, Marta stands out as a gem of culinary excellence where every dish tells a story of tradition and passion. Owned by Chef Matthew Oetting, a New York chef with over twenty years of dedicated culinary refinement, Marta is a celebration of authentic Italian flavors brought to life with a modern twist. Marta not only highlights their food and drinks, but have also created a place where locals gather, celebrate, and create memories, truly embodying the Italian philosophy of 'good food shared with great company.'

name: Cypriana - Homewood
description: For parties of 5 or more, please call us at 410-837-7482. From our very conception, Cypriana has been and will always be committed to providing clean, house-made and authentic Cypriot cuisine. A cuisine that is an amalgamation of the world’s wealthiest cultures. And we consider it a privilege to have started in one of the oldest cities in America. We predicate our image and pride on the familial hang-out environment and refined dishes that you can only get at Cypriana. It is our desire to bring a more traditional approach to full-service dining.
